questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: We can separate a mixture of iron filings from sulphur by using a magnet because …
questions.explanation: Iron is attracted to magnets, so when you pass a magnet over a mixture of iron filings and sulphur, the iron filings will stick to the magnet while the sulphur stays behind. This works because iron has magnetic properties that allow it to be pulled by magnets. Sulphur is non-magnetic, but that's not the main reason we can separate them – the key is that iron IS magnetic, making it easy to pick out of the mixture.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What does the symbol below represent in an electric circuit?
questions.explanation: An open switch is shown by a break or gap in the circuit symbol, which means the circuit is not complete and electricity cannot flow through it. When a switch is closed, the symbol shows a complete connection with no gap, allowing current to pass through. Think of it like a bridge - when the bridge is broken (open), you cannot cross, but when it's complete (closed), you can travel across it.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The part labelled A is the …
questions.explanation: The effort is the force you apply to make a lever work, and part A shows where you push or pull on the lever to move something. While the whole object is indeed a lever, part A specifically marks the point where your force is applied. Think of opening a bottle with a bottle opener - your hand pushes at one spot (the effort), and that's different from the tool itself or where the bottle cap sits.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following levers can he use?
questions.context_text: George wanted to lift a heavy rock.
questions.explanation: A crowbar is the best lever to lift a heavy rock because it works like a strong arm that helps you push down on one end to lift something heavy on the other end. While a wheelbarrow is also a lever, it is designed to carry and move things from one place to another, not to lift heavy objects off the ground. A crowbar gives you the most lifting power when you need to get underneath and raise a heavy rock.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following factors can increase the risk of a lightning strike?

options.text: Standing under a tree during a thunder storm
Not swimming during a thunder storm
Lying down during a thunder storm
Not touching metal objects during a thunder storm

questions.explanation: Lightning looks for the tallest objects to strike, so standing under a tree during a thunderstorm is very dangerous because the tree attracts lightning and you could be struck too. Lying down is actually safer than standing because you make yourself smaller and less likely to be hit. The other options are all good safety actions that help protect you from lightning, not things that increase your risk.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The following are transparent objects except …
questions.explanation: Transparent objects are materials that allow light to pass through them so you can see clearly through to the other side. Wood is not transparent because it blocks light completely, making it impossible to see through it. Windscreens, clear glass, and glass bottles all let light pass through them, which is why you can see through these objects.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following instruments does not use refraction of light?
questions.explanation: A thermometer works by measuring how liquids expand or contract with temperature changes, not by bending light rays. Lenses, telescopes, and binoculars all use refraction, which is when light bends as it passes through curved glass to help us see things more clearly or make them look bigger. The thermometer simply shows us the level of liquid inside it, so no light bending is needed for it to work.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a method of heat transfer?
questions.explanation: Heat moves from hot places to cold places in three main ways: conduction (through touching), convection (through liquids and gases moving), and radiation (through rays like sunlight). Convection is when heated liquid or air rises and cooler parts sink down, creating a movement that transfers heat—like when you see steam rising from hot water. Condensation is actually when water vapor turns into liquid, which is a completely different process and not a way heat travels.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What substance do we use to tell if something is alkaline or neutral?
questions.explanation: An indicator is a special substance that changes color to show us whether something is alkaline, acidic, or neutral. Litmus paper is actually one type of indicator, so it might seem like a good answer, but indicators include many other substances like universal indicator solution and red cabbage juice that can test for alkaline or neutral substances. Think of "indicator" as the general name for all substances that help us identify acids and alkalis!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What does the experiment prove?
questions.context_text: Wame set up the experiment. Water boiled where the source of heat is and the ice did not melt.
questions.explanation: When water is heated from the top, the hot water stays at the top because convection causes hot liquids to rise naturally, so the ice at the bottom doesn't melt. This experiment proves convection in liquids because if heat moved through the water by conduction (option B), the ice would have melted since conduction spreads heat evenly through liquids. The setup cleverly shows that convection currents only work when you heat from below, not from above.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following set-ups can she use to soften hard water?
questions.context_text: Lesego was asked to soften hard water which was collected from the stream.
questions.explanation: Hard water contains dissolved minerals like calcium and magnesium, and washing soda works by chemically reacting with these minerals to remove them from the water, making it soft. While boiling can remove some hardness, it only works for temporary hardness and doesn't remove all the minerals like washing soda does. Filtration and sedimentation only remove solid particles that you can see, but they cannot remove dissolved minerals that make water hard.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The instrument illustrated below is used to measure?
questions.explanation: An anemometer is the special instrument that measures how fast the wind is moving, which we call wind speed - it has cups that spin faster when the wind blows harder. While option A might seem close because the instrument does involve wind, it's not complete enough because we need to know the specific measurement it gives us, which is the speed of that wind in units like kilometres per hour. The spinning cups don't measure air pressure or temperature at all - those require completely different instruments.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following activities shows friction in our daily lives?
questions.explanation: When we walk, our shoes push against the ground and the ground pushes back, creating friction that stops us from slipping - without this friction, we would slide around like on ice! Activities like crying, watching, or reading don't involve surfaces rubbing together, so they don't create friction. Friction is a force that happens when two surfaces move against each other, which is exactly what happens between your feet and the ground when you walk.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following illustrations represents a series electric circuit?
questions.explanation: In a series circuit, there is only one path for electricity to flow through, which means all the components are connected one after another in a single loop. Circuit C shows this arrangement where the current must pass through each component in turn with no branches or alternative routes. If you see a circuit with multiple paths or branches where the current can split up, that would be a parallel circuit, not a series circuit.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: One night when Thato was getting ready for bed she saw some sparks getting off her clothes. The sparks were the result of … electricity which was produced.
questions.explanation: When you rub clothes together, they can build up charges that stay in one place until they suddenly jump as a spark - this is called static electricity. Current electricity is different because it flows continuously through wires in a circuit, like the electricity that powers your lights at home. The sparks you see when getting undressed are a quick release of static charge that built up from friction.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: A fine powder mixed with water and shaken well. The mixture looked milky. It is called a …
questions.explanation: When a fine powder is mixed with water and creates a milky appearance, it means the particles are floating throughout the water but have not dissolved—this is called a suspension. While it is true that this is a type of mixture, the more specific and correct scientific term is suspension because the powder particles remain separate and will eventually settle at the bottom if left to stand. A solution would be clear, not milky, because in solutions the particles completely dissolve and disappear into the liquid.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens at a cold front?

options.text: Cold air sinks and warms up

questions.explanation: At a cold front, a mass of cold air moves in and pushes underneath the warm air that was there before, so cold air replaces warm air. While it's true that warm air does rise when the cold air pushes under it (which is why option C might seem correct), that's what happens during the process, not what a cold front actually means – a cold front is specifically about cold air taking the place of warm air in an area.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The type of force that exists between surfaces as they rub against each other is called …
questions.explanation: When two surfaces rub against each other, like when you push a desk across the floor or rub your hands together, the force that resists this movement is called friction force. This force always acts to slow down or stop the rubbing motion between the surfaces. Gravitational force is different because it pulls objects downward toward the Earth, not sideways between rubbing surfaces.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: A flock of guinea fowl that live near your cattle post is called …
questions.explanation: A population is a group of the same kind of animal living together in one area, so all those guinea fowl near your cattle post form a population. You might think it's a species, but species refers to the type or kind of animal itself (guinea fowl), not the actual group of birds living in that place. A community would include all the different types of living things in that area, like the guinea fowl, the cattle, grass, and other animals together.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is refraction?

options.text: When light travels through an object
When a light ray is bent
When a light ray travels in a straight line
When a light ray bounces back

questions.explanation: When light passes from one material to another (like from air into water), it changes speed and bends at an angle - this bending is called refraction. You might confuse this with option A, but light travelling through an object is just transmission, not refraction - the key is that refraction specifically means the light ray bends when it enters a different material. This is why a straw looks bent when you put it in a glass of water!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which scientific process do scientists use to state how and when something is likely to happen in the future?
questions.explanation: When scientists use evidence and patterns they've observed to say what will happen in the future, they are predicting. For example, scientists predict when the rainy season will come or when a plant will flower based on data they've collected. A hypothesis is different because it's just an educated guess that scientists make before they test something, while predicting uses information we already know to forecast future events.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is true about boiling and evaporation?

options.text: Boiling: takes place at all temperatures; Evaporation: takes place at a fixed temperature
Boiling: takes place at a fixed temperature; Evaporation: takes place at all temperatures
Boiling: takes place only at the surface of the liquid; Evaporation: takes place throughout the liquid
Boiling: takes place inside the fridge; Evaporation: takes place at all temperatures

questions.explanation: Boiling happens only when water reaches exactly 100°C (its boiling point), which is why we say it occurs at a fixed temperature. Evaporation is different because it can happen at any temperature - you can see this when wet clothes dry in the sun or even on a cool day, because water molecules can escape from the surface whenever they have enough energy. Some students confuse these processes and think boiling happens at any temperature because they see steam rising from warm water, but that's actually evaporation, not boiling!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following illustrations shows a crescent moon?
questions.explanation: A crescent moon looks like a thin, curved slice or banana shape, with only a small part of the moon lit up by the sun. Illustration B shows this characteristic crescent shape with just a small curved portion visible. Some other moon phases might look similar, but they show more of the moon's surface lit up, making them fuller shapes like a half-circle or nearly full circle.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the chemical symbol for lead?
questions.explanation: Lead's chemical symbol is Pb, which comes from its old Latin name "plumbum" – this is why many chemical symbols don't match their English names! You might have thought it was Ld because that matches the English word "lead," but scientists use Latin names for many elements. Hg is actually mercury, and Cu is copper.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Washing soda is …
questions.explanation: Washing soda, also known as sodium carbonate, is a strong alkali with a high pH that helps clean clothes by breaking down grease and removing stains effectively. It is definitely not an acid at all, so options A and C are wrong – acids and alkalis are complete opposites on the pH scale. While some cleaning products are weak alkalis, washing soda is particularly powerful, which is why it works so well at softening hard water and getting your clothes really clean.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following substances are correctly classified?

options.text: Element: Iron; Compound: Sodium chloride; Mixture: Sand and sugar
Element: Copper; Compound: Zinc; Mixture: Sulphur and iron filings
Element: Hydrogen; Compound: Chloride; Mixture: Pepper and salt
Element: Oxygen; Compound: Gold; Mixture: Sodium chloride

questions.explanation: An element is a pure substance made of only one type of atom like iron, a compound is two or more elements chemically joined together like sodium chloride (salt), and a mixture is substances physically mixed together but not chemically joined like sand and sugar. Option B is incorrect because zinc and copper are both elements, not compounds – remember, compounds must have different elements bonded together! Option D wrongly classifies gold as a compound when it's actually an element, while sodium chloride is a compound, not a mixture.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which method of heat transfer is represented in the diagram?
questions.explanation: Heat energy can travel through empty space without needing any material to carry it, and this process is called radiation - just like how the Sun's heat reaches Earth through space! Conduction and convection both need matter (solid, liquid, or gas) to transfer heat from one place to another, so they cannot work through empty space. The diagram must be showing radiation because it's the only method that works without direct contact between objects or movement of particles.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Gaone pulled an object but it does not move at all. Which statement is correct?

options.text: There is no force against the object
The friction force is greater than the pulling force
The force of the pull is very strong

questions.explanation: When Gaone pulls the object but it doesn't move, it means the friction force pushing against her pull is stronger than her pulling force. If there were no forces against the object (option A) or if friction was zero (option D), the object would easily slide when she pulled it. Remember, for an object to stay still, the forces must be unbalanced with friction being the stronger force in this case.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a strong acid?
questions.explanation: Sulphuric acid is a strong acid because it is a powerful chemical that can cause serious burns and damage materials very quickly, which is why it's used in car batteries and factories. Lemon juice, fruit juice, and vinegar are all weak acids that we can safely eat and drink because they only have a mild acidic effect on our tongues, making them taste sour but not dangerous to touch or taste.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is not an impact of rubber tyres on the transport system?
questions.explanation: Rubber tyres actually help vehicles move faster and smoother because they grip the road well and absorb bumps, making rides more comfortable. The size of a vehicle depends on how much space is needed inside for passengers or cargo, not on whether it has rubber tyres or not. Rubber tyres do cause vehicles to use slightly more fuel because of friction with the road, but this is much better than the old metal or wooden wheels that made travel very slow and uncomfortable.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: She chose the lowest power rating because she wanted to …
questions.context_text: Dikatso went to an electronic shop to buy an electric kettle and decided to buy the one with the lowest power rating.
questions.explanation: A lower power rating means the electric kettle uses less electricity to do its job, which helps save energy and reduces the electricity bill. When we conserve electricity, we use only what we need and avoid wasting it, which is better for our homes and the environment. Power rating tells us how much electricity an appliance uses, not how well it works, so choosing a lower rating is about using less power, not improving it.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following objects allows heat to pass through?
questions.explanation: Metals are excellent conductors of heat, which means they allow heat to pass through them easily - this is why metal pots get hot quickly when cooking and why metal feels cold when you touch it (it conducts heat away from your hand). Materials like plastic, wood, and rubber are insulators that block or slow down heat from passing through, which is why we use them for pot handles and electrical wire coverings to protect us from heat and electricity.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Although gases are poor conductors, heat can still be carried through them by …
questions.explanation: Heat moves through gases mainly by convection, which happens when warm gas rises and cool gas sinks, creating a circular movement that carries heat from one place to another. While conduction does happen in gases, it works very poorly because gas particles are far apart and don't bump into each other often enough to transfer much heat. This is why convection is the main way heat travels through air and other gases.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: A molecule of a compound can contain …

options.text: Two atoms of the same element
Atoms of many different kinds of elements

questions.explanation: A compound is formed when atoms of different elements join together, like how water (H₂O) contains both hydrogen and oxygen atoms. A molecule of a compound can have many different kinds of elements combined in various amounts - for example, sugar has carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen atoms all bonded together. Remember that if a molecule has only one kind of element, like O₂ (oxygen gas), it's not a compound but rather an element in molecular form.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: A lunar eclipse can occur …

options.text: During any phase of the moon
Only when it is a new moon
Only when it is a full moon

questions.explanation: A lunar eclipse happens when Earth moves between the Sun and the Moon, casting Earth's shadow on the Moon. This can only occur during a full moon because that's when the Moon is on the opposite side of Earth from the Sun, allowing Earth's shadow to fall on it. During a new moon, the Moon is between Earth and the Sun, which is when a solar eclipse can happen instead.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following shows a food chain?
questions.explanation: A food chain shows the direction energy flows from one living thing to another, starting with a producer (like grass) and moving through consumers with arrows pointing toward the eater. Option A is correct because grass makes its own food, the grasshopper eats the grass, the frog eats the grasshopper, and the snake eats the frog—the arrows show who gets eaten by whom. Option D might look tempting, but the arrows point backwards, showing energy flowing the wrong way from predators back to their prey, which doesn't happen in nature.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: We say water is soft if …

options.text: It contains dissolved magnesium and calcium salts
Soap does not lather well in it
It does not contain dissolved salts
It causes scale to form in pipes

questions.explanation: Water is considered soft when it is pure and does not have minerals like calcium and magnesium salts dissolved in it. Hard water is the opposite – it contains these dissolved salts, which is why soap doesn't lather well in it and it causes scale to form in kettles and pipes. So remember, soft water is clean and free from these dissolved minerals, making it much better for washing and cleaning!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Heat from the sun reaches the earth through …
questions.explanation: Heat from the sun travels through the empty space between the sun and Earth, where there are no particles to bump into each other or carry the heat. This type of heat transfer is called radiation, which doesn't need any material or air to travel through – it can move through empty space just like light does. Conduction and convection both need particles or materials to touch and transfer heat, which is why they cannot work in the empty space of outer space.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The name given to a group of birds living together in a given area is …
questions.explanation: A population is a group of the same type of organism living together in the same area, so a group of birds in one place is called a population. An ecosystem would include not just the birds, but also other animals, plants, and non-living things like water and soil in that area. Remember, population means one type of organism, while community means different types of organisms living together.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When a light beam shines onto a plane mirror …

options.text: The incoming light ray is called the incident ray
The light ray that bounces back is called the refracted ray
The light ray is bent through refraction
The light beam passes through the mirror

questions.explanation: When light hits a mirror, we call the incoming beam of light the incident ray - this is the scientific term for light traveling towards the mirror. The light ray that bounces back is called the reflected ray, not the refracted ray, because mirrors reflect light rather than bend it. Refraction only happens when light passes through different materials like water or glass, but mirrors bounce light back instead of letting it pass through.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Animals are called consumers because they …
questions.explanation: Animals are called consumers because they cannot make their own food like plants do, so they must eat other living things to get energy. Plants are called producers because they can make their own food using sunlight, water, and air, but animals must consume (eat) plants or other animals to survive. Even though some animals only eat plants, they are still consumers because they depend on eating other organisms rather than producing food themselves.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which instrument is used to measure humidity?
questions.explanation: A hygrometer is the special instrument we use to measure humidity, which is the amount of water vapor (moisture) in the air - this is important for understanding weather conditions and how comfortable we feel. You might think a thermometer would measure humidity since wet air can feel different, but thermometers only measure temperature, not moisture in the air.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When electric charge flows from one place to another it is called …
questions.explanation: When electric charges flow or move from one place to another through a wire or conductor, we call this current electricity because the charges are flowing like a current in a river. Static electricity is different - it happens when charges build up and stay in one place without moving, like when you rub a balloon on your hair and it sticks to the wall. Think of current electricity as charges on the move, powering your torch or radio!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is TRUE about hazards associated with lightning?

options.text: It cannot be used to protect the house
A wooden beam cannot catch fire

questions.explanation: Lightning is a powerful electrical discharge that carries tremendous energy, which can definitely damage buildings by causing fires, cracking walls, or destroying electrical systems when it strikes. This is why many buildings have lightning rods installed on their roofs – these metal rods actually do protect houses by safely directing the lightning's electricity into the ground instead of letting it damage the structure. A wooden beam can easily catch fire if struck by lightning because wood burns when exposed to the intense heat produced by a lightning strike.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which scientific innovation is correctly matched with its discoverer?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone in 1876, which revolutionized how people communicate over long distances. You might have been tempted by option D, but Thomas Edison invented the light bulb and phonograph, not antibiotics—it was actually Alexander Fleming who discovered antibiotics like penicillin, though he's incorrectly listed with pasteurisation in option A, which was Louis Pasteur's invention.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is the best tool to use when observing a solar eclipse?
questions.explanation: Looking directly at the sun during a solar eclipse can seriously damage your eyes, so you need special protection that blocks harmful rays. Welding glasses are specially designed to protect eyes from extremely bright light and are safe for viewing eclipses, while regular sunglasses and even binoculars or telescopes without proper filters can still let in dangerous amounts of sunlight that will harm your eyes. Remember, never look at the sun through regular tools meant for normal viewing!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The process shown above by the arrows is known as …
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the process where water moves up through a plant and evaporates from the leaves into the air, which is exactly what the arrows are showing in the diagram. While evaporation also involves water turning into water vapour, it refers to water changing from surfaces like ponds or soil, not specifically the water loss from plants through their leaves. Think of transpiration as plants "breathing out" water vapour as they take in carbon dioxide for photosynthesis.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which electrical wiring is best in our homes?
questions.explanation: In our homes, we use parallel circuits because each electrical appliance can work independently - when one light bulb goes off, the others stay on, and each device gets the full voltage it needs to work properly. A series circuit would be a poor choice because if one appliance stops working or is switched off, all the others would stop too, just like old Christmas lights where one broken bulb makes the whole string go dark. Parallel wiring is safer and more practical for homes, which is why electricians always install it this way.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which type of energy is used to generate electricity at Morupule power station?
questions.explanation: Morupule power station burns coal to produce heat, which is then used to generate electricity, making it a thermal power station. Even though Botswana has plenty of sunshine, Morupule doesn't use solar panels – instead, it relies on our country's coal deposits found near Palapye. The heat from burning coal creates steam that turns turbines to produce the electricity we use in our homes and schools.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following nails will rust in the containers shown?
questions.explanation: Iron nails need both water and oxygen from the air to rust, which is why the nail in water rusts because water contains dissolved oxygen. The nail in dry air might seem like it should rust since air has oxygen, but without moisture or water, rusting cannot happen. Greased nails and nails in oil are protected because the grease and oil form a barrier that keeps both water and oxygen away from the metal surface.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which process takes place at P in the diagram?
questions.explanation: At point P, water from the ocean or lake is heated by the sun and changes from liquid water into water vapour (gas) that rises into the air - this process is called evaporation. While transpiration also adds water vapour to the air, it specifically happens when plants release water through their leaves, not when water evaporates directly from bodies of water like we see at P.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which one of the following is correct? Sound waves …
questions.explanation: Sound needs something to travel through, and it moves by making molecules vibrate and bump into each other, passing the sound energy along like dominoes falling. This is why sound cannot travel through a vacuum (empty space) where there are no molecules at all, and also why sound can actually travel through water – that's how whales and dolphins communicate! Light is much faster than sound, which is why you see lightning before you hear the thunder.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When a stick is put into water it looks as if the stick is bent. This is because light is …
questions.explanation: When light travels from air into water, it bends or changes direction, and this bending is called refraction. This is why the stick appears bent at the point where it enters the water - the light rays coming from the underwater part of the stick bend as they leave the water and reach your eyes. Reflection is different because it happens when light bounces off surfaces like mirrors, but refraction is the bending that occurs when light passes through different materials.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Green plants convert the sun's energy into …
questions.explanation: Green plants use sunlight to make their own food through photosynthesis, and they store this energy as chemical energy in sugars like glucose. While the sun provides light energy and some heat is produced during the process, plants actually convert and store the energy in a chemical form that they can use later for growth and other life processes. This chemical energy is what we get when we eat plants or animals that have eaten plants!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Heat is conducted very poorly in …
questions.explanation: Heat moves through materials when their particles touch and bump into each other, passing energy along. In gases, the particles are very far apart from each other, so they rarely collide and heat transfers very slowly. Solids like iron bars conduct heat much better because their particles are tightly packed together and constantly touching, making it easy for heat energy to pass from one particle to the next.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which one of the following statements is NOT TRUE?

options.text: An acid will not change the colour of red litmus paper
An alkali will turn red litmus paper blue
Pure water will change red litmus paper to blue
Handy Andy will not change the colour of blue litmus paper

questions.explanation: Pure water is neutral, which means it is neither an acid nor an alkali, so it will not change the colour of any litmus paper at all. Remember that only alkalis turn red litmus paper blue, while acids keep red litmus paper red and turn blue litmus paper red. Since pure water is neutral, it leaves both red and blue litmus paper unchanged.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following could be the reason for the car slowing down?
questions.context_text: A car is rolling along a level road with its engine running and it starts slowing down.

options.text: There is a weak wind blowing from behind the car
There is friction between the car's tyres and the road
There is no friction at all

questions.explanation: When a car slows down on a level road, friction between the tyres and the road surface works against the car's movement, causing it to lose speed. This friction force opposes motion and is always present when two surfaces rub together. A weak wind from behind would actually help push the car forward rather than slow it down, and having no friction at all would mean the car couldn't grip the road to stop or even move properly in the first place.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Sheet lightning …

options.text: Passes from a cloud to the earth
Will not occur when it is raining

questions.explanation: Sheet lightning is the bright flash you see lighting up the sky when lightning occurs between clouds or behind clouds, making the whole cloud appear to glow like a sheet of light. Some people think sheet lightning is silent, but it does make thunder – you just might not hear it because it happens so far away from where you are standing. This is different from fork lightning, which you can see as a jagged bolt traveling from a cloud down to the ground.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The best way to protect electric equipment during a thunderstorm is to …

options.text: Stand it on a rubber mat

questions.explanation: During a thunderstorm, lightning can send extremely powerful electrical surges through power lines and into your home, which can damage or destroy plugged-in equipment even if it's switched off. The only way to truly protect your electric equipment is to completely disconnect it from the wall socket, because this breaks the path that the lightning's electricity would travel. Simply switching equipment off still leaves it connected to the dangerous power surge that could come through the wires.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: If one atom of oxygen combines with two atoms of hydrogen, it forms a …
questions.explanation: When one oxygen atom joins with two hydrogen atoms, they form water (H₂O), which is a molecule of a compound because it contains two different types of atoms chemically bonded together. This is not a molecule of an element because elements contain only one type of atom, like O₂ (oxygen gas) or H₂ (hydrogen gas). It's also not a mixture because the atoms are chemically joined, not just physically mixed together like sand and salt.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Sadie passed a light beam through a solid object. The object blocked the light beams from passing through it. What conclusion can be drawn? The object was:
questions.explanation: When an object completely blocks light from passing through it, we call that object opaque. Materials like wood, metal, and thick cardboard are opaque because no light can travel through them at all. Transparent objects like clear glass would let light pass straight through, while translucent objects like frosted glass would let some light through but make things look blurry.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Alec was stirring water in a glass using a pencil. This is what he observed when he rested the pencil on the glass. The conclusion that can be drawn from the above experiment is that there was:
questions.explanation: When light travels from water into air, it bends and changes direction, making the pencil appear broken or bent at the water's surface – this bending of light is called refraction. Reflection is different because it happens when light bounces off a surface like a mirror, showing you an image of something, but here the pencil itself looks bent because light is passing through the water and bending. This is why objects underwater always look like they're in a different position than they really are!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Acids have a … taste
questions.explanation: Acids have a sour taste, which you can notice in foods like lemons, oranges, and vinegar - they all make your mouth pucker! Sweet tastes come from sugars, not acids, while bitter tastes are usually found in alkaline substances like baking soda or very strong tea. Remember though, you should never taste chemicals in the laboratory to test if they are acids - we use special indicator papers for that instead!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a principle of energy conservation?

options.text: Energy is the ability to do work
Energy cannot be created or destroyed but it can be converted from one form to another
Energy can be created or destroyed
Energy can only be created but cannot be converted from one form to another

questions.explanation: Energy conservation means that energy always stays the same amount in total – it cannot be created from nothing or destroyed completely, but it can change from one form to another, like when electrical energy becomes light and heat in a bulb. Option A describes what energy is, not how it is conserved, while options C and D are wrong because energy can never be created or destroyed at all. This principle helps us understand that energy is always transformed, never lost, which is why we can trace where energy goes in any system.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is correctly matched?

options.text: Strong acid: Tartaric acid; Weak acid: Hydrochloric acid
Strong acid: Hydrochloric acid; Weak acid: Tartaric acid
Strong acid: Sulphuric acid; Weak acid: Nitric acid
Strong acid: Vinegar; Weak acid: Sulphuric acid

questions.explanation: Strong acids like hydrochloric acid and sulphuric acid break apart completely in water to release lots of hydrogen ions, making them very powerful and dangerous, while weak acids like tartaric acid (found in grapes) and vinegar only release some hydrogen ions, making them gentler and safer to handle. You might be confused by option C because both sulphuric acid and nitric acid are actually strong acids, not weak ones, so they cannot be matched together as opposites. Remember that common household acids like vinegar and citric acid are usually weak, while laboratory acids with "hydro-" or "-ic" names like hydrochloric and sulphuric are typically strong.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When a blue litmus paper is dipped in an acid it …
questions.explanation: Blue litmus paper is a special indicator that changes color to show us whether a substance is an acid or a base. When you dip blue litmus paper into an acid, it always turns red because acids have a special property that makes blue litmus change to red. If the litmus paper stays blue, that tells you the substance is not an acid—it's either a base or neutral like water.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The following are protected animals in Botswana EXCEPT …
questions.explanation: Baboons are common animals in Botswana and are not on the protected species list, which means they can be hunted with proper permits. Rhinos, cheetahs, and kori bustards are all protected by law because their numbers are low and they are at risk of disappearing from our country. The government protects these special animals to make sure future generations can still see them in Botswana.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is a saturated solution?

options.text: A solution that cannot dissolve any solute anymore

questions.explanation: A saturated solution is one that has dissolved as much solute as it possibly can at that temperature - if you try to add more solute, it will just sink to the bottom without dissolving. Think of it like a sponge full of water that cannot absorb even one more drop! Options B and C might sound correct because we sometimes use "strong" and "weak" to describe solutions, but these words actually refer to concentration (how much solute is dissolved), not whether the solution can still dissolve more.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How can you soften hard water?
questions.explanation: When you boil hard water, the heat causes the dissolved minerals (like calcium) to form a solid coating on the pot, which removes them from the water and makes it softer. Using more or less soap doesn't change the minerals in the water - it only changes how much soap you're wasting, since hard water makes it difficult for soap to lather properly. Remember, softening water means removing the minerals that make it hard, not just using different amounts of water or soap!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a traditional scientific practice used in Botswana?
questions.explanation: Making clay pots is a traditional scientific practice in Botswana because it involves understanding natural materials like clay soil, knowing the right temperature for firing, and applying skills that have been passed down through generations in our culture. This is real science that our ancestors used every day! Making metal pots requires different technology that wasn't traditionally available in Botswana, while making cake and tea are cooking activities rather than traditional scientific practices.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What can be used to separate sand and salt?
questions.explanation: To separate sand and salt, you first dissolve the salt in water so it mixes completely while the sand doesn't dissolve. Then you use filtration to pour the mixture through a filter paper, which traps the sand particles but lets the salty water pass through. While dissolving is an important first step, filtration is the actual separation method that removes the sand from the mixture.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following methods can be used to separate iron fillings from the soil?
questions.explanation: Iron is a magnetic material, which means it is attracted to magnets, while soil is not magnetic. When you hold a magnet near the mixture, the iron fillings will stick to the magnet and separate from the soil easily. Sieving might seem like it could work, but iron fillings and soil particles are often similar in size, so they would pass through the sieve holes together instead of separating.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which chemical element is correctly matched with its symbol?
questions.explanation: Lead is a heavy metal element that has been used for thousands of years, and scientists use the symbol Pb because it comes from lead's Latin name "plumbum" (which is also why people who fix water pipes are called plumbers!). You might think copper should be C, but copper's symbol is actually Cu (from its Latin name "cuprum"), while C is the symbol for carbon, and the other symbols Au and Zn belong to gold and zinc respectively.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What energy changes take place when you use a battery to play a radio?
questions.explanation: When you play a radio using a battery, the chemicals inside the battery store energy that gets converted into electrical energy, which then powers the radio's speaker to produce sound energy. This is why the answer is chemical energy to sound energy – the battery's stored chemical energy ends up as the sound you hear from the radio. Light energy would only be correct if the battery was powering something that produces light, like a torch or bulb, not a radio that makes sound.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How does heat from the sun reach us?
questions.explanation: Heat from the sun travels through empty space to reach Earth by radiation, which is the only way energy can move through a vacuum where there is no air or matter. Convection and conduction both need materials like air or solid objects to transfer heat, but space between the sun and Earth is empty, so these methods cannot work. Radiation allows the sun's energy to travel as invisible waves that warm our planet when they arrive.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: … is when the heat travels through a vacuum?
questions.explanation: Radiation is the only way heat can travel through a vacuum because it moves as waves that don't need any material to pass through, just like how the Sun's heat reaches Earth through empty space. Conduction and convection both need particles of matter (like air or water) to transfer heat, so they cannot work in a vacuum where there is nothing at all.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a correct statement?

options.text: Climate is the condition of the atmosphere over a long period of time whereas weather is an average weather conditions for 100 years
Climate is the average weather condition of a place over a long period of time whereas weather is a condition over a short period of time
Climate is the condition of the atmosphere over a short period of time whereas weather is a condition of atmosphere over a long period of time
Climate is the average condition of a place over a short period of time whereas weather is a condition of a place for 30 years

questions.explanation: Weather describes what's happening in the atmosphere right now or over a few days, like if it's hot and sunny today in Gaborone. Climate, on the other hand, is the pattern of weather we expect in a place when we look at many years of information – for example, we know Botswana generally has a hot, dry climate because that's what the weather has been like here over many, many years. Think of it this way: weather is what you wear today, but climate is what types of clothes you keep in your wardrobe all year long.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Plants convert light energy from the sun into …
questions.explanation: Plants use sunlight to make their own food through photosynthesis, and they store this energy as chemical energy in substances like glucose (sugar). This chemical energy is what keeps the plant alive and growing, and it's also the energy we get when we eat plants or animals that ate plants. While plants might feel warm in the sun, they don't convert light into heat energy as their main purpose – they convert it into chemical energy that can be stored and used later.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following conducts heat from the list?
questions.explanation: Solids are the best conductors of heat because their particles are tightly packed together, allowing heat energy to pass quickly from one particle to the next, like when a metal spoon gets hot in tea. While liquids and gases can also transfer heat, they do so much more slowly because their particles are farther apart and move around more freely. Light is not a state of matter at all, so it doesn't conduct heat the same way materials do.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is population?

options.text: Group of animals or plants of the same species
Place where plants and animals live

questions.explanation: A population is made up of living things that are all the same kind - like a group of zebras in the savanna or a stand of mopane trees growing together. The key is that they must all be the same species, not different types of animals or plants mixed together. Option D describes a habitat, which is where populations live, but not what a population actually is.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which phase shows full moon?
questions.explanation: A full moon occurs when the entire face of the moon that we see from Earth is lit up by the Sun, appearing as a complete bright circle in the sky. Phase B shows this perfect round, fully illuminated moon. Some other phases might show the moon partly lit, like a crescent or half-circle, but only when the whole face is bright do we call it a full moon.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: A hurricane …

options.text: A wind which forms within a low pressure system over ocean areas between the tropics
Where cold air replaces warm air
An area of low pressure caused by warm air rising into the atmosphere
A large rotating severe storm that forms within a low pressure system over ocean areas between the tropics

questions.explanation: A hurricane is a powerful, spinning storm system that develops when warm ocean water heats the air above it, creating a low pressure area where winds begin rotating in a massive circle. While option A mentions wind and low pressure over the ocean, it's incomplete because a hurricane is much more than just wind—it's an entire severe storm system with heavy rain, thunder, and dangerous winds all rotating together. Think of it like a giant spinning wheel of bad weather that can be hundreds of kilometres wide!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: A front is …

options.text: A place where air masses combine
A boundary between different types of masses of air

questions.explanation: A front in weather happens when two different air masses meet each other - for example, when warm air meets cold air. Think of it like two different types of air trying to push against each other at a boundary line, just like when you see clouds forming in a line across the sky. While option B might seem close, the air masses don't actually combine or mix together - they stay separate and push against each other at the boundary, which is why we get changes in weather at fronts.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Mr Morake was travelling along a road on a very hot day and saw something like a pool of water some meters ahead. That incident is caused by …
questions.explanation: When hot air rises from the road surface on a very hot day, light rays bend as they pass through layers of air at different temperatures, creating what we call refraction of light - this makes Mr Morake see a mirage that looks like water on the road ahead. While reflection of light involves light bouncing off surfaces like mirrors, what causes this mirage is actually light bending through the hot air, not bouncing off anything. This is why the "water" disappears as you get closer to it!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The name of the diagram above is …
questions.explanation: A food chain is a simple diagram that shows how energy and food passes from one living thing to another in a straight line, like grass → zebra → lion. A food web is different because it shows many connected food chains with arrows going in different directions, showing that animals often eat more than one type of food. Since this diagram shows just one pathway of who eats whom, it is a food chain.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: In a lever of second class …

options.text: Pivot is between the load and the effort
Load is between the pivot and the effort
Load is at one end of the lever
Effort is between the pivot and the load

questions.explanation: In a second class lever, the load is always positioned between the pivot (fulcrum) and the effort, which makes it easier to lift heavy things - think of a wheelbarrow where the wheel is the pivot, the load sits in the middle, and you lift the handles with effort. Option A describes a first class lever like a seesaw, where the pivot is in the middle instead. This arrangement in second class levers gives you a mechanical advantage, meaning you can lift heavy loads with less effort!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which one is a switch on the diagram below?
questions.explanation: A switch is the component in a circuit that can open or close to control whether electricity flows through the circuit, just like a light switch on your wall at home. In the diagram, A is the switch because it shows the part that can be turned on or off to complete or break the electrical path. The other components (B, C, and D) are different parts of the circuit like the battery, bulb, or wires that serve other purposes.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following places is the most dangerous to be during a thunder storm?

options.text: In a hole in the ground

questions.explanation: Lightning follows the easiest path to the ground, so it strikes the tallest objects first, making tall trees very dangerous because you could be struck if you stand under them. Inside a house or car, you are protected because the electricity travels around the outside of these structures, and even a hole in the ground is safer because you are lower than your surroundings where lightning might strike.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Where is the pivot in the pair of scissors below?
questions.explanation: The pivot is the point where the two blades of the scissors are joined together by a screw or bolt, allowing them to move around that fixed point. In these scissors, point O is where the two blades are connected and rotate, making it the pivot or fulcrum. Point P might look important because it's where you apply force with your fingers, but remember the pivot must be the fixed turning point, not where you push.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The best way to protect electric equipment during a thunderstorm is to …

options.text: Stand it on a rubber mat

questions.explanation: During a thunderstorm, lightning can send powerful electrical surges through the wires in your home, which can damage any equipment that's plugged in. Disconnecting equipment completely (unplugging it from the wall) breaks the path between the dangerous lightning surge and your device, keeping it safe. Simply switching equipment off isn't enough protection because electricity from lightning can still travel through the plugged-in cord and damage the equipment even when it's turned off.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The traditional way of making fire is through …
questions.explanation: When you rub two sticks together very quickly, friction creates heat between them, and this heat can eventually start a fire - this is how our ancestors made fire for thousands of years! Pressure alone won't create the heat needed for fire because you need the rubbing motion that friction provides. You can try this yourself by rubbing your hands together quickly - notice how they get warm from friction!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following can make magnets lose their magnetism?

options.text: Keeping them separated with non-magnetic material

questions.explanation: When you drop a magnet from a height, the hard impact can shake up the tiny magnetic particles inside it, causing them to lose their organized arrangement and making the magnet weaker or non-magnetic. The other options about keeping magnets separated or in containers are actually good storage practices that help preserve magnetism, not destroy it. Physical shock from dropping, heating magnets very hot, or hitting them with a hammer are the main ways magnets lose their magnetic power.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How can one reduce friction on objects?
questions.explanation: Rollers reduce friction because they change sliding motion into rolling motion, which creates much less resistance between surfaces – think of how much easier it is to move heavy furniture on wheels than to drag it across the floor! Dragging, pushing, and rubbing all create more friction because they involve surfaces sliding directly against each other, which makes movement harder and requires more force.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The following are streamlined objects EXCEPT?
questions.explanation: Streamlined objects have smooth, curved shapes that help them move easily through air or water by reducing friction and air resistance. Aeroplanes, fish, and kites all have these special curved shapes that let them glide smoothly, but a monkey has a rough, bumpy body shape with arms and legs sticking out that create lots of resistance when moving. That's why engineers copy streamlined shapes from nature when designing fast-moving vehicles!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a strong acid?
questions.explanation: Strong acids like hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, and sulphuric acid completely break apart in water and are very powerful, which is why they are commonly used in laboratories and industries. Tartaric acid is a weak acid found naturally in fruits like grapes and tamarinds - it doesn't break apart completely in water, making it safe enough to be used in foods and drinks. Remember, weak acids are still acids with a sour taste and can turn blue litmus paper red, but they are much gentler than strong acids.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Metsi wants to cook dumplings. She pours two cups of water in a pot then places a dish or bowl in the pot with water and cooks. Which method of heat transfer is she using?
questions.explanation: When Metsi heats the water in the pot, the hot water moves around in currents, carrying heat to the bowl or dish sitting in it - this moving of heat through liquids is called convection. You might think it's conduction because the bowl touches the water, but conduction only works when objects directly touch each other without any movement, while here the water itself is moving and transferring the heat as it flows around the bowl.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The following are factors increasing the risks of lightning strikes EXCEPT …

options.text: Walking bare footed in the rain
Putting on boots during rainy time

questions.explanation: Lightning is attracted to metal objects and tall things that stick up high, so wearing rubber boots actually protects you during a storm because rubber does not conduct electricity well. Standing under tall trees is very dangerous because lightning often strikes the tallest objects, and walking barefoot or touching metal wires puts you at risk since your body can become a path for the electrical current to reach the ground.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following statement about career in science is true?

options.text: All science careers can be pursued by males and females
All science careers are for males only
All careers are for females only
All science careers are difficult to pursued

questions.explanation: Science is for everyone, no matter if you are a boy or a girl! Both males and females can become doctors, engineers, botanists, chemists, or any other science professional they choose. While some people wrongly believe that only boys should do science careers, the truth is that girls and boys are equally capable of succeeding in any science field they work hard to pursue.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Sound travels quickly in …
questions.explanation: Sound travels fastest through solids because the particles are very close together and tightly packed, allowing sound vibrations to pass quickly from one particle to the next. In gases like air, the particles are far apart, so sound moves much slower as it has to jump bigger gaps between particles. Sound cannot travel at all through a vacuum because there are no particles to carry the vibrations.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which lever does the effort lie between load and pivot?
questions.explanation: In a second class lever, the effort is at one end, the pivot (fulcrum) is at the other end, and the load sits between them, like when you use a wheelbarrow where you lift the handles (effort) while the wheel acts as the pivot and the load is in the middle. A first class lever is different because the pivot is in the middle, with the effort and load on opposite ends, like a seesaw. Remember the order for second class levers: Pivot-Load-Effort, which makes it easier to lift heavy things!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the type of force that exists between surfaces as they rub against each other?
questions.explanation: When two surfaces rub against each other, like when you push a book across your desk, friction force is created that tries to slow down or stop the movement. This is different from magnetic force, which only works between magnets and certain metals without needing to touch, or gravitational force, which pulls objects toward Earth. Friction is the special force that always appears when surfaces are in contact and moving against each other.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is true about alkaline?
questions.explanation: Alkaline substances have a bitter taste, which is one of their special properties that helps us identify them - think about the bitter taste of soap if you've ever accidentally gotten it in your mouth! This is very different from acids, which have a sour taste like lemons or vinegar. Remember though, we should never taste chemicals in science class to identify them because many can be dangerous!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: A place where organism stay is known as …
questions.explanation: A habitat is the specific place where an organism lives and finds everything it needs to survive, like food, water, and shelter. For example, a mopane worm's habitat is a mopane tree, and a lion's habitat is the grassland. An ecosystem is different because it includes the habitat plus all the living things and how they interact with each other in that area.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which electrical wiring is the best in our homes?
questions.explanation: In our homes, we use parallel circuits because each electrical appliance gets its own separate path to the power source, so if one device stops working or is switched off, all the others keep running normally. This is much better than a series circuit, where all devices share one path and switching off one device would turn off everything in your home – imagine your TV going off every time someone turns off a light! Parallel wiring also means each appliance gets the full voltage it needs to work properly.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is the best way of observing a solar eclipse?
questions.explanation: Looking directly at the sun during a solar eclipse can permanently damage your eyes, so you need very strong protection like welding glasses, which are specially designed to block harmful sun rays. Regular sunglasses might seem safe because they make things darker, but they don't block enough of the dangerous rays that can burn your eyes. Binoculars and telescopes would actually make it worse by magnifying the sun's harmful rays directly into your eyes!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which type of energy is used to generate electricity at Morupule power station?
questions.explanation: Morupule power station burns coal to produce heat, which creates steam to turn turbines and generate electricity – this is called thermal energy. While hydroelectric power stations use water from dams to make electricity, Morupule uses coal because Botswana has large coal deposits near Palapye but doesn't have big rivers with enough water flow for hydroelectric dams. The heat from burning coal is what makes Morupule a thermal power station.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When substances turn a red litmus paper blue, they are said to be …
questions.explanation: Substances that turn red litmus paper blue are alkaline (also called bases), because alkaline substances have this special property of changing the colour of litmus paper from red to blue. Acids do the opposite – they turn blue litmus paper red, which is how we can tell them apart. Remember this simple rule: alkaline substances make red litmus go blue, while acids make blue litmus go red!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Mavis added sugar in her tea and stirred it. Some of the sugar did not dissolve even if she tried hard. What conclusion can you make about the solution?
questions.explanation: When sugar can no longer dissolve in tea even after stirring, the solution has become saturated, which means the tea is holding as much sugar as it possibly can at that temperature. The sugar did dissolve at first, but once the solution reached its limit, the extra sugar remained at the bottom as solid crystals. A saturated solution is full and cannot accept any more solute.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The situation where Neo sees a pool of water on the road that disappears when she comes closer is called …
questions.explanation: A mirage is an optical illusion that happens when hot air near the road surface bends light rays, making it look like there's water ahead that disappears as you get closer. This is different from refraction, which is the scientific name for light bending, but we use the special word "mirage" to describe this particular desert or hot road illusion. You might have seen this effect on very hot days on the roads around Botswana!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which group of animals are cold blooded and lays eggs in water?
questions.explanation: Amphibians are cold-blooded animals that must lay their eggs in water because their eggs have no hard shell and would dry out on land. Animals like frogs, toads, and salamanders are amphibians that start their lives in water as tadpoles before growing legs and moving onto land. Although reptiles like crocodiles and turtles are also cold-blooded and some lay eggs near water, they lay their eggs on land, not in the water itself.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Lighting occurs because of the presence of …
questions.explanation: Lightning happens when static electricity builds up in clouds and suddenly jumps between the clouds and the ground, creating a bright flash of light and heat. This is different from current electricity that flows steadily through wires in your home to power lights and appliances. The huge spark you see during a thunderstorm is nature's way of releasing the static charge that has built up, just like the small shock you might feel when touching a metal door handle after walking on a carpet.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The following are ways of reducing friction EXCEPT …
questions.explanation: Friction happens when surfaces rub together, and we can reduce it by making surfaces smoother or separating them. Increasing roughness does the opposite – it makes surfaces grip more and creates MORE friction, like how rough sandpaper creates more resistance than smooth paper. Ball bearings, oiling, and streamlining all work to reduce friction by making movement smoother and easier.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is refraction?

options.text: When a light ray is bent
When light travels in a straight line
When light travels through an object

questions.explanation: Refraction happens when light moves from one material to another, like from air into water, and the light ray bends or changes direction. You might have noticed this when a straw in a glass of water looks bent or broken at the surface. Option A describes reflection, which is different because that's when light bounces back off a surface like a mirror, rather than bending as it passes through.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a property of acidic substances?

options.text: Substances which feel soapy when touched

questions.explanation: Acidic substances have a sour taste, which is why lemons, vinegar, and sour milk all taste sharp or tangy - they contain acids! While some acids can be corrosive (able to wear away materials), not all acids are strongly corrosive, so this is not their main identifying property. Remember, substances that feel soapy are actually the opposite of acids - they are alkalis or bases!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Why parallel circuits are most preferred in buildings …

options.text: If there is break in one current flow other flow current are affected too
If there is break down in one current flow currents are not affected

questions.explanation: Parallel circuits are preferred in buildings because each appliance has its own separate path for electricity to flow. This means if one light bulb breaks or one appliance stops working, all the other lights and appliances keep working normally. In a series circuit (which would make option B true), if one device breaks, everything stops working - imagine if your entire house went dark just because one bulb broke!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The gas used by animals to breathe in is …
questions.explanation: Animals breathe in oxygen from the air because their bodies need it to release energy from food in a process called respiration. When animals breathe out, they release carbon dioxide, which is the waste gas produced during respiration - so carbon dioxide goes OUT of the body, not IN. Although nitrogen makes up most of the air we breathe in, our bodies don't actually use it the way they use oxygen.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: An ecosystem …

options.text: Can contain living and non-living things
Contains things that do not depend on one another

questions.explanation: An ecosystem is like a natural community that includes both living things (like plants, animals, and insects) and non-living things (like water, soil, air, and sunlight) all working together. All these parts depend on one another – for example, plants need sunlight and water to grow, and animals need plants for food. So an ecosystem cannot contain only living things or only non-living things, because both are needed for life to survive.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Setshwantsho tried to look through a certain object and he was able to see clearly through it. It was …
questions.explanation: When you can see clearly through an object, like looking through a clean window or a glass of water, that object is transparent because it lets light pass straight through without blocking your view. Translucent objects might seem similar, but they only let some light through in a blurry way, like frosted glass or wax paper, so you cannot see clearly through them. Opaque objects like wood or metal block all light completely, so you cannot see through them at all.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Materials that are not able to conduct electricity are known as …
questions.explanation: Materials that do not allow electricity to flow through them are called insulators because they block or insulate against the movement of electrical current. Conductors are the opposite – they are materials like metals that do allow electricity to pass through them easily. Wood and nails are just examples of materials, where wood is an insulator and a nail (being metal) is a conductor.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which instrument is used to measure humidity?
questions.explanation: A hygrometer is the special instrument we use to measure humidity, which tells us how much water vapor is in the air around us. You might confuse it with a thermometer, but remember that a thermometer only measures temperature (how hot or cold it is), not the moisture in the air. A barometer measures air pressure, while a rain gauge collects and measures rainfall that has already fallen from the sky.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a traditional way of conserving animals?
questions.explanation: National Parks and Game Reserves are traditional ways that Botswana and other countries have used for many years to protect animals by setting aside special protected areas where wildlife can live safely. While hunting seasons, conservation laws, and education are all modern methods of animal conservation, they came later as new approaches to help protect our wildlife. Setting aside land as protected areas for animals was one of the very first methods our ancestors used to make sure wildlife would survive for future generations.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following changes of states of matter is represented by letter S? (GAS → S → LIQUID)
questions.explanation: When a gas cools down and changes into a liquid, we call this process condensation – think of water droplets forming on the outside of a cold drink bottle on a hot day. Freezing might seem correct, but that's when a liquid turns into a solid (like water becoming ice), not when a gas turns into a liquid. The letter S represents condensation because it shows the change from gas to liquid state.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which one of the following is NOT an environmental benefit of conservation to Botswana?

options.text: Rare plant and animal species are able to survive
Plants reduce carbon dioxide in the atmosphere
We are conserving our natural heritage for future generations

questions.explanation: Conservation means actively protecting and managing our environment, but it doesn't mean the environment is never damaged - some harm can still occur even with conservation efforts. The real benefits of conservation include protecting rare animals like our rhinos and wild dogs, helping plants absorb carbon dioxide to keep air clean, and ensuring that future generations of Batswana can enjoy our beautiful natural resources like the Okavango Delta.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: During a full moon …

options.text: The moon shines in the afternoon

questions.explanation: During a full moon, the Sun lights up the entire side of the Moon facing Earth, so we see the whole Moon looking bright and round in the night sky. You might think half the Moon is dark because sometimes we do see half-moon shapes, but that happens during different phases when the Moon is in other positions—not during a full moon. The Moon is always brightest at night when it's full, not in the afternoon when the Sun is still shining brightly.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which element is represented by the symbol Pb?
questions.explanation: Lead is the element represented by the symbol Pb, which comes from its Latin name "plumbum." This is why some elements have symbols that don't match their English names. Phosphorus might seem like a good choice because it starts with "P," but its symbol is actually just P, not Pb.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following weather symbols is NOT matched with its description?
questions.explanation: Looking at weather symbols, snow is the one that is NOT matched correctly with its description in this question. Snow has a specific symbol (usually shown as snowflakes *), which is different from other weather symbols like clouds, wind arrows, or lightning bolts. The other options like very cloudy, storm wind, and thunderstorm all have distinct symbols that match their weather conditions correctly.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How do living things interact with the soil?

options.text: Without soil people can live in an environment
Animal do not eat the soil so it does not affect them
People and animals depend on plants which grow from the soil

questions.explanation: Living things and soil work together in an important cycle - plants grow in soil and get nutrients from it, then people and animals eat those plants (or eat other animals that ate plants) to survive. This means we all depend on soil even though we don't eat it directly, just like how animals depend on it through the food chain. Soil isn't killed by living things; instead, it stays healthy when dead plants and animals break down and return nutrients back to it.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a chemical element?
questions.explanation: Carbon is a pure chemical element because it is made up of only one type of atom and cannot be broken down into simpler substances. Carbon dioxide, water, and table salt are all compounds, which means they are made by combining two or more different elements together chemically. You can remember this by noticing that compounds have names that suggest they contain more than one element, like carbon di-oxide (carbon + oxygen) or H₂O for water (hydrogen + oxygen).

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens to the boiling point of water when salt is added?

options.text: The boiling point does not change
The boiling point increases and decreases at the same time

questions.explanation: When you add salt to water, the salt particles get in the way of the water molecules trying to escape as steam, so the water needs to get hotter before it can boil. This means the boiling point increases above the normal 100°C. Some students might think the boiling point stays the same because pure water always boils at 100°C, but adding salt changes this by making the water impure.

questions.subject: science
