questions.question_text: In an experiment, sugar and iron filings were added to two different containers. After some minutes the sugar particles disappeared while iron filings remained. We say iron filings are …
questions.explanation: When a substance dissolves in water, it breaks down into tiny particles that we cannot see anymore, like the sugar did. Iron filings do not dissolve in water, so they stay visible at the bottom of the container – we call this property insoluble. Some students might confuse this with soluble (option B), but soluble means the opposite – it describes substances that DO dissolve and disappear in water, like sugar or salt.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which organism is a producer?
questions.context_text: Use the food web to answer question 11.
questions.explanation: Producers are organisms that make their own food from sunlight through photosynthesis, and maize is a green plant that does exactly this. Chickens, snakes, and vultures are all consumers because they must eat other organisms to get energy - they cannot make their own food like plants can. Remember, in any food web, only plants and other photosynthetic organisms can be producers.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following helps substances to dissolve faster?

options.text: Using a substance with large particles

questions.explanation: Hot water makes substances dissolve faster because the heat gives the water molecules more energy to move around quickly and break apart the particles of the substance you're dissolving. Cold water works too, but much more slowly since the water molecules move less energetically. Think about how quickly sugar dissolves in your hot tea compared to a cold drink!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following does NOT explain how the wheel invention helped in development of transport system?

options.text: Vehicles travel at a very low speed nowadays
It is easier to turn a light wheel
Vehicles with rubber wheels are much quieter

questions.explanation: The invention of the wheel helped transport develop by making vehicles faster, more comfortable, easier to control, and quieter with rubber wheels. Option B is incorrect because vehicles today actually travel at very high speeds, not low speeds – this is one of the great benefits the wheel brought to transport! All the other options correctly explain how the wheel improved our transport systems over time.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When does a lunar eclipse occur?

options.text: Only when it is near the moon
Only when it is a full moon
During any phase of the moon

questions.explanation: A lunar eclipse happens only during a full moon because that's when the Earth moves directly between the Sun and the Moon, casting Earth's shadow on the Moon. The Moon must be on the opposite side of Earth from the Sun for this alignment to work, which is exactly what a full moon is. While you might think an eclipse could happen during any moon phase, the three objects need this special straight-line arrangement for Earth's shadow to fall on the Moon.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: At which position will a lunar eclipse occur?
questions.explanation: During a lunar eclipse, the Earth must be directly between the Sun and the Moon, blocking sunlight from reaching the Moon's surface. Looking at position S, you can see the Earth is perfectly lined up between the Sun and Moon, which is exactly what creates a lunar eclipse. Position Q might look tempting because the Moon is between the Sun and Earth, but that arrangement actually creates a solar eclipse, not a lunar eclipse.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following does NOT lather easily?
questions.explanation: Sea water contains high amounts of dissolved salts and minerals like calcium and magnesium, which make it hard water that does not form lather easily with soap. Rain water, river water, and distilled water are all soft water types with very few dissolved minerals, so they form lather quickly and easily when soap is added.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is meant by the word population?

options.text: Animals or plants of the same species that are found in a certain place at that time
Different plants that live together in a certain area
Animals or plants of different species that live together in a certain area

questions.explanation: A population is a group of living things that are all the same species living together in one area, like a group of impala in the Okavango Delta or a group of mopane trees in your village. It's important to remember that a population must be the same species - not different species mixed together. When you have different species living together in the same area, that is called a community, not a population.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is an effect of pollution on the environment?
questions.explanation: Global warming is an effect of pollution on the environment because when harmful gases from factories, cars, and burning waste go into the air, they trap heat around the Earth and make our planet warmer. While pollution can also cause eye problems and breathing problems in people, these are effects on human health, not on the environment itself. The question asks specifically about effects on the environment, which includes things like climate, oceans, forests, and wildlife.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What contribution did Thomas Edison make in Science?
questions.explanation: Thomas Edison was a famous American inventor who created the first practical electric light bulb in 1879, which changed how people could light their homes and work at night. While Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone, Edison's light bulb was his most important contribution to science because it brought electricity into everyday life for millions of people around the world. His invention helped make modern life possible by allowing us to have light whenever we need it, not just during daytime.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following human activities leads to water pollution?

options.text: Playing a radio at high volumes
Poisonous gases released into the atmosphere
Throwing leftovers and other harmful materials everywhere on land

questions.explanation: Water pollution happens when harmful substances get directly into water bodies like rivers, dams, and streams, making the water dirty and unsafe for drinking or for animals. When you throw waste directly into water, it pollutes the water immediately, but throwing waste on land (option D) causes land pollution instead, even though some of that waste might eventually wash into water during rains. Playing loud radios and releasing gases into the air cause noise pollution and air pollution, not water pollution.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following describes a warm front?

options.text: Air that has the same temperature and amount of moisture
The boundary between different types of masses of
Where warm air replaces cold air
Where cold air replaces warm air

questions.explanation: A warm front forms when a mass of warm air moves forward and pushes up over cooler air, replacing the cold air that was there before. Option D might seem correct, but that describes a cold front, which is the opposite situation where cold air pushes under and replaces warm air. Think of it this way: the type of front is named after which air mass is advancing or taking over the area.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a traditional scientific practice used in Botswana?
questions.explanation: Beef processing is a modern industrial practice that uses machines and factories, not a traditional scientific method passed down through generations in Botswana. Traditional scientific practices like fermentation (making madila or bojalwa), preservation (drying meat for biltong), and dyeing (making natural colors from plants) have been used by our ancestors for hundreds of years using their knowledge of nature and science. These traditional methods show how our people understood science long before modern laboratories existed!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which human activity can cause air pollution?
questions.explanation: Burning fuels like petrol, coal, and wood releases smoke and harmful gases into the air, which directly causes air pollution. While cutting down trees is harmful to the environment because trees help clean the air by absorbing carbon dioxide, the act of cutting them down doesn't actually release pollutants into the air itself. Burning is the activity that puts dangerous particles and gases directly into the air we breathe.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the name of the process labelled 12?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the special process where plants release water vapour through tiny holes in their leaves called stomata, which is different from regular evaporation happening from soil or water surfaces. While evaporation might seem correct because both involve water turning into vapour, transpiration specifically refers to water loss from living plants as part of the water cycle. Think of it this way: when you see label 12 pointing to a plant, you know water is escaping through the leaves, not just evaporating from any surface.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How does tourism benefit people who live around the Chobe National Park?
questions.explanation: Tourism at Chobe National Park creates many jobs for local people who work in hotels, lodges, and campsites as cooks, cleaners, tour guides, and managers. These are positive ways that people earn money to support their families while helping visitors enjoy our beautiful wildlife. The other options describe negative activities that are illegal and do not show how tourism actually benefits communities living near the park.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes weather?

options.text: The condition of atmosphere which only occurs in summer
The condition of atmosphere at a particular place over a short period of time
The average weather condition of a place over a long period of time
The average weather condition of a place that takes place when the air is humid

questions.explanation: Weather describes what the atmosphere is like right now or over a few days at a specific place - like whether it's hot, cold, rainy, or windy today in Gaborone. Option C might seem correct, but it actually describes climate, which is the pattern of weather over many years (like how Botswana is generally dry and hot). Weather changes quickly from day to day, while climate tells us what to expect over a long time.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which organism is correctly classified?
questions.explanation: A goat eats grass and other plants, which makes it a primary consumer because it gets its energy directly from producers (plants). Grass is actually a producer because it makes its own food through photosynthesis, not a tertiary consumer. Dogs are also consumers that eat other organisms, not producers, and buffalos are primary consumers like goats since they only eat plants, not secondary consumers that would eat other animals.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What observation will Mothusi make?
questions.context_text: Mothusi mixed sand with hot water and stirred. Then he mixed salt with hot water and stirred.

options.text: Sand: Dissolves; Salt: Does not dissolve
Sand: Does not dissolve; Salt: Does not dissolve
Sand: Does not dissolve; Salt: Dissolves

questions.explanation: When Mothusi stirs sand in hot water, the sand particles are too large to break apart and mix with water, so they will sink to the bottom - we say sand does not dissolve. However, when he stirs salt in hot water, the salt breaks down into tiny particles that spread throughout the water and disappear, which means the salt dissolves completely.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which statement best describes a front?

options.text: A large, rotating, severe storm that forms within a low pressure system
Where cold air replaces warm air
An area of high air pressure caused by cold air sinking towards the earth's surface
The boundary between different types of masses of air

questions.explanation: A front is the boundary line where two different air masses meet, like when warm tropical air from the north meets cooler air from the south over Botswana. While option B might seem correct because cold air replacing warm air is one type of front (called a cold front), this is just one example, not the complete definition—fronts can also be where warm air replaces cold air or where air masses meet without replacing each other. Think of a front as the "border" between different types of air, just like the borders between countries.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How can a mixture of pepper and sand be separated?
questions.explanation: Pepper is very light and can easily stick to objects with static electricity, while sand is too heavy to be attracted by a static charge, so you can use a charged object like a plastic ruler rubbed on cloth to pick up the pepper and leave the sand behind. Dissolving and filtering won't work because neither pepper nor sand dissolve in water, and a magnet won't help since neither material is magnetic. This method works great because pepper and sand have different weights and pepper responds to electrical charges!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The best way to look at a solar eclipse is to use …
questions.explanation: Looking directly at the sun during a solar eclipse can seriously damage your eyes, so you need very strong protection that blocks harmful rays. Welding glasses are specially made with dark filters strong enough to protect your eyes from the sun's dangerous light, while regular sunglasses are far too weak and won't keep your eyes safe. Never use telescopes or binoculars to look at the sun because they actually make the sun's rays stronger and can cause instant, permanent blindness.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following substances is a compound?
questions.explanation: Table salt is a compound because it is made when two different elements, sodium and chlorine, join together chemically to form something new with different properties. Carbon, oxygen, and potassium are all elements because they are made of only one type of atom and cannot be broken down into simpler substances by chemical means. Remember, compounds always contain two or more different elements bonded together!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is true about gender and science careers?

options.text: Science careers are for both men and women
Science careers are only for men
Science careers are only for women
Science careers are only for old people

questions.explanation: Science careers welcome everyone regardless of gender because both men and women have the same ability to learn, discover, and solve problems through science. Throughout history and today in Botswana, we see successful scientists of all genders making important discoveries in medicine, technology, agriculture, and environmental protection. Your interest and hard work in science matter much more than whether you are male or female.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which organism is a producer?
questions.explanation: Producers are organisms that make their own food using sunlight through photosynthesis, and sorghum is a plant that does exactly this. Animals like snakes, lions, and eagles are consumers because they must eat other organisms to get energy - they cannot make their own food from sunlight. Plants are the only true producers in nature because they can turn sunlight, water, and air into food for themselves and other living things.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is an antibiotic?

options.text: Heating milk to a certain temperature and cooling it immediately
A medicine that kills bacteria but does not harm the person
A substance that can change the colour of several materials

questions.explanation: Antibiotics are special medicines that doctors give you when you have an infection caused by bacteria - they kill the harmful bacteria in your body without hurting your own cells. Option A might sound correct because antibiotics do help protect you from disease, but that describes vaccines, which prevent you from getting sick in the first place, while antibiotics treat bacterial infections you already have. Option B describes pasteurisation, which is a process used to make milk safe to drink by killing germs with heat.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Marang added too much sugar in water until there was no more of it dissolving. Marang's solution is …
questions.explanation: When you keep adding sugar to water until no more sugar can dissolve and some remains at the bottom, you have made a saturated solution – the water is holding as much sugar as it possibly can at that temperature. An unsaturated solution would still be able to dissolve more sugar if you added it, but Marang's solution cannot do this anymore because it has reached its maximum capacity.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which term refers to the natural home where plants and animals live?
questions.explanation: A habitat is the natural home or place where specific plants and animals live, just like how you live in your house in your village or town. While an ecosystem might sound similar, it refers to all the living things in an area plus the non-living things like water and soil working together, which is much bigger than just a home. Think of a habitat as the address where an animal lives, like a bird's nest in a tree or a fish's home in a river.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: One of the modern ways of protecting wild animals in Botswana is through …
questions.explanation: National Parks and Game Reserves are modern ways of protecting wild animals because they create safe, protected areas where animals can live without being hunted or disturbed, and trained rangers can monitor and care for them. While beliefs, taboos, and totems are traditional methods that our ancestors used to protect certain animals, today we rely on official protected areas like Chobe National Park and the Okavango Delta where laws keep wildlife safe. These parks also allow us to enjoy watching our beautiful wildlife while ensuring they survive for future generations!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a way of collecting rain water?
questions.explanation: Bridges are structures built to help people and vehicles cross over rivers, valleys, or roads, so they are not designed to collect or store rainwater. Reservoirs, tanks, and dams are all specially designed to catch and hold water, including rainwater, so we can use it later for drinking, farming, and other important needs. Remember, in Botswana where water is precious, we use these storage methods to make sure we have enough water during dry seasons.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What kind of eclipse is shown by the diagram below?
questions.explanation: When the Moon passes directly between the Sun and Earth, blocking the Sun's light from reaching Earth, we call this a solar eclipse. The diagram shows this arrangement with the Moon casting a shadow on Earth. A lunar eclipse happens the other way around—when Earth blocks sunlight from reaching the Moon—so the positions would be different.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the process through which the best of growing things are chosen?
questions.explanation: When farmers and gardeners want to grow the best plants or breed the best animals, they carefully choose the healthiest and strongest ones to reproduce – this process is called selection. Distillation might sound scientific, but it's actually about separating liquids by heating them, like making traditional bojalwa or purifying water. Selection helps us get better crops, stronger cattle, and healthier plants over time by choosing only the best ones for breeding.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When children learn about wildlife by watching their natural surroundings, they are benefiting from conservation …
questions.explanation: Learning about wildlife by watching nature around you is a cultural benefit because it helps pass down traditional knowledge and connects you to your community's way of life, just like learning stories from elders. While this learning does help the environment, the question asks about the benefit to the children themselves – they are gaining cultural knowledge and understanding of their heritage. This type of learning strengthens your identity and helps you appreciate the wisdom that has been passed down through generations in Botswana.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Who discovered vaccinations?
questions.explanation: Edward Jenner discovered vaccinations in 1796 when he used material from cowpox sores to protect a boy from the deadly smallpox disease, creating the world's first vaccine. While Louis Pasteur later developed other important vaccines like the rabies vaccine, Jenner was the pioneer who started vaccination as we know it today. Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin (an antibiotic), which is different from vaccines because it treats infections rather than prevents them.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which animal is mostly protected in Botswana?
questions.explanation: Rhinoceroses receive the most protection in Botswana because they are critically endangered and very few are left in the wild, making them targets for illegal poaching. Our government and wildlife organizations work hard to guard rhinos with armed anti-poaching units and special security measures. While animals like kudu and zebra are protected in our game reserves, they have much larger populations and don't face the same risk of extinction as rhinos do.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the statements below is true about soft water?

options.text: Does not lather well with soap

questions.explanation: Soft water is wonderful for washing because it lathers well with soap, creating lots of bubbles easily, which means you need less soap to get things clean. Hard water is the opposite – it's the one that uses a lot of soap, doesn't lather well, and leaves that white chalky fur on kettles and pots because it contains minerals like calcium. When you have soft water in your home, bathing and washing dishes becomes much easier and more economical!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which definition best describes suspension?

options.text: A liquid that mixes with solid
A substance that dissolves in water
A mixture of solid particles dissolving in a liquid, making the liquid look milky
A mixture of solid particles floating in a liquid, making the liquid look milky

questions.explanation: A suspension is when tiny solid particles float around in a liquid without dissolving, which makes the liquid appear cloudy or milky, like when you mix sand in water or shake up muddy water. The particles in a suspension don't dissolve like sugar does in tea - instead they stay as separate pieces that can settle to the bottom if you leave the mixture standing for a while. This is different from dissolving, where the solid completely disappears into the liquid and won't settle out.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which one is an effect of hard water?
questions.explanation: Hard water contains minerals like calcium and magnesium that react with soap to form a scum instead of lather, which means you need to use much more soap to get things clean - this wastes soap! This is actually the opposite of saving money or using less soap, because you have to buy more soap when you have hard water. The minerals in hard water don't affect how quickly it boils.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: From the following, which one is a habitat of fish?
questions.explanation: Fish need water to survive, and a dam is a place where water collects, making it a perfect habitat where fish can live, swim, and find food. Hills, mountains, and trees are on land and don't have the water that fish need to breathe through their gills and move around.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is likely to happen to plants and animals in an area that is experiencing drought?

options.text: More animals will come to the area

questions.explanation: During a drought, there is not enough water for plants to grow well, and animals struggle to find water and food to survive. When living things cannot get what they need, many will die or move away to find better conditions, which causes the population to decrease. The population cannot increase or stay the same because drought makes it harder, not easier, for plants and animals to live and reproduce in that area.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is not a great scientist?
questions.explanation: Alexander Forbes is not a famous scientist, while the other three people made groundbreaking discoveries that changed the world. Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone, Thomas Edison created the light bulb, and Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin, which saves millions of lives by fighting infections. These three are celebrated as great scientists because their inventions and discoveries help people every day.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The plant that is protected throughout Botswana is …
questions.explanation: The Baobab tree is specially protected by law throughout Botswana because it is a national heritage tree that takes hundreds of years to grow and provides food, shelter, and medicine for people and animals. While Mophane trees are also very important and common in Botswana, they are not protected by law in the same way as the Baobab. Sorghum is a farm crop that farmers plant each year, so it doesn't need special protection like our ancient Baobab trees do.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which career requires the application of science and technology?
questions.explanation: A dentist uses science to understand how teeth and gums work, and applies technology like X-ray machines, drills, and special materials to treat patients' dental problems. While journalists and interpreters are important careers, they rely mainly on communication skills rather than scientific knowledge and medical equipment. Dentists must study biology, chemistry, and use advanced tools to keep our teeth healthy!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When a scientist forecasts that something will happen in the future they are …
questions.explanation: When a scientist uses information and evidence to say what will happen in the future, they are making a prediction - just like predicting if it will rain tomorrow based on dark clouds in the sky. While scientists do communicate their ideas to others, communicating means sharing information, not specifically telling what will happen next. Predicting is the special word we use when someone makes an informed guess about future events based on what they know.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a difference between boiling and evaporation?

options.text: Boiling takes place throughout the liquid while evaporation takes place only at the surface of the liquid
Boiling takes place at any temperature while evaporation takes place at a fixed temperature
Boiling takes place in gases while evaporation takes place in solids
Boiling takes place only at night while evaporation takes place only during the day

questions.explanation: When water boils in a pot, you can see bubbles forming throughout all the water, even at the bottom, because boiling happens everywhere inside the liquid at once. Evaporation is different - it only happens at the top surface of the water, which is why puddles can slowly dry up even on cool days without boiling. Remember that both boiling and evaporation change liquid water into water vapor (gas), but they happen in different parts of the liquid.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which gas makes up 21% of air?
questions.explanation: Air is a mixture of different gases, and oxygen makes up about 21% of it - this is the gas we breathe in to stay alive! You might have thought it was nitrogen because that gas actually makes up most of the air (about 78%), but nitrogen is much more common than oxygen in our atmosphere.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When salt is added to ice, its melting point …
questions.explanation: When you add salt to ice, the salt breaks apart the ice structure and makes it harder for water molecules to freeze together, so the ice melts at a lower temperature than normal (below 0°C). This is why we sometimes put salt on icy roads in cold countries - it helps the ice melt even when the air temperature is still freezing! The melting point doesn't stay the same because the salt actually changes what's happening between the water molecules.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of these is one of the first four inner planets of the solar system?
questions.explanation: The four inner planets closest to the Sun are Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars, which are all rocky planets that form the inner solar system. Saturn, Jupiter, and Uranus are much larger gas giant planets that orbit farther away from the Sun in the outer solar system. Mars is correctly called an inner planet because it sits between Earth and the asteroid belt that separates the inner planets from the outer ones.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The animals with no back bone are called …
questions.explanation: Animals are divided into two main groups based on whether they have a backbone or not. Invertebrates are animals without a backbone, like insects, spiders, and worms, while vertebrates are animals with a backbone, like humans, fish, and birds. Carnivores and herbivores describe what animals eat (meat or plants), not whether they have backbones, so these are incorrect choices.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Hard water can be softened by …
questions.explanation: Washing soda is a special chemical that removes the minerals (like calcium and magnesium) that make water hard, turning it into soft water that creates a better lather with soap. Using less soap doesn't actually change the hardness of the water - it just means you're using less soap with hard water, which will still give you poor washing results. Adding salt would actually make the water worse for washing, not better!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the phase of the moon shown in the diagram below?
questions.explanation: Looking at the diagram, you can see the moon appears as a complete, bright circle, which means we are seeing the entire sunlit side of the moon from Earth - this is called a full moon. The quarters (first and second) would only show half of the moon's face lit up, while a new moon would appear completely dark because the sunlit side faces away from us.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Science careers are …
questions.explanation: Science careers are for everyone, regardless of whether you are male or female, and you can start preparing for them while you are still young by studying hard in school. Both men and women work as doctors, engineers, botanists, and researchers all over Botswana and the world, and they all began learning science when they were students just like you. Age doesn't matter either – you start building your science career through your education now, not when you are old.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: One of the following is not a great scientist. Who is he?
questions.explanation: Thomas Fleming is not a famous scientist because he is not a real person who made important discoveries. The other three are all real scientists: Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone, Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin (an important medicine), and Alexander Forbes studied the nervous system. You might have been confused because Alexander Fleming sounds similar to Thomas Fleming, but only Alexander Fleming is the actual scientist.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which organism is correctly classified?
questions.explanation: Grass is correctly classified as a producer because it makes its own food using sunlight through photosynthesis, just like all green plants do. A cow cannot be a producer because it eats grass to get energy rather than making its own food, which makes it a consumer (specifically a herbivore). Remember, only organisms that can make their own food from sunlight, like plants, are producers in the food chain.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is not a traditional way of conserving animals?
questions.explanation: Hunting big predators like lions, leopards, and cheetahs actually reduces animal populations and harms the ecosystem, so it is not a way of conserving animals. Traditional conservation methods like totems, beliefs and taboos protected certain animals by making it forbidden to kill them, which helped keep those animal populations safe for future generations. Hunting all year round might sound wrong, but some communities traditionally hunted throughout the year in controlled, sustainable ways that didn't threaten animal populations.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The plant that is protected throughout Botswana is …
questions.explanation: The Mophane tree is legally protected throughout Botswana because it is so valuable to our country - it provides food for mopane worms, which many people depend on for income and nutrition, and its wood is used carefully for many purposes. While the Baobab is also an important and protected tree in Botswana, it is the Mophane that has special protection laws covering the entire country. Both trees are precious to our environment, but Mophane's protection is nationwide due to its economic and ecological importance.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The modern way of protecting wild animals in Botswana is through …
questions.explanation: National parks and game reserves are the modern way we protect wild animals in Botswana because they provide safe, protected areas where animals can live without being hunted or disturbed, and trained rangers can monitor and care for them. While beliefs, taboos, and totems were traditional ways our ancestors protected animals and are still important culturally, today we rely on official protected areas like Chobe National Park and the Central Kalahari Game Reserve to keep our wildlife safe for future generations.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which animal is mostly protected in Botswana?
questions.explanation: Wild dogs are mostly protected in Botswana because they are endangered animals with very small populations left in Africa, and our country has one of the largest remaining groups of them in the world. While zebras and kudus are also wild animals found in Botswana, they have much larger populations and are not as threatened with extinction, so they don't need the same level of special protection. Cows are domestic farm animals that people raise for food and milk, so they don't need wildlife protection.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How do communities around protected areas benefit from tourism?

options.text: They earn money from tourists by selling curios

questions.explanation: When tourists visit protected areas like game reserves, they buy handmade crafts, baskets, and other curios from local communities, which helps these families earn money to support themselves. This is one of the main ways conservation helps people living near wildlife areas. While communities might sometimes guide tourists as part of their work, simply walking with tourists doesn't provide the regular income that selling goods does.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How do we describe a situation where no more solute dissolves in water after enough of it has already dissolved?
questions.explanation: When water has dissolved as much solute as it possibly can and no more solute will dissolve, we call this a saturated solution - think of it like a sponge that is completely full of water and cannot absorb any more. An unsaturated solution, on the other hand, still has room to dissolve more solute, just like a dry sponge can still absorb more water. The word "saturated" means completely full or soaked, which perfectly describes this situation.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which definition best describes suspension?

options.text: A mixture of solid particles floating in a liquid
A liquid that mixes with solid
A substance that dissolves in water
A mixture of solid particles not dissolving in liquid

questions.explanation: A suspension is when solid particles float or hang in a liquid without dissolving, like when you shake sand in water and the sand particles spread throughout but don't disappear. While option D sounds similar because it mentions particles not dissolving, the key feature of a suspension is that the particles actually float around in the liquid rather than just sitting at the bottom. Think of muddy water from the Limpopo River – the dirt particles stay mixed in the water for a while before settling down.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes climate?

options.text: The condition of atmosphere which only occurs in summer
The condition of atmosphere at a particular place over a short period of time
The average weather condition of a place over a long period of time
The average weather condition of a place that takes place when the air is humid

questions.explanation: Climate is the pattern of weather that a place experiences over many years, usually 30 years or more, which is why we say Botswana has a semi-arid climate with hot summers and mild winters. Option B might seem correct, but it actually describes weather, not climate – weather is what happens day-to-day or week-to-week, like today's temperature or yesterday's rain. Think of it this way: weather is what you wear today, but climate is the types of clothes you keep in your wardrobe all year round.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following equipment is suitable to use when watching a solar eclipse?
questions.explanation: Looking directly at the sun during a solar eclipse can permanently damage your eyes, so you need special eclipse goggles that are designed to block harmful solar rays while letting you view the eclipse safely. Regular sunglasses, binoculars, and telescopes are not safe because they don't block enough of the sun's dangerous light, and binoculars or telescopes actually make it worse by concentrating the sun's rays and burning your eyes even faster.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which statement best describes weather?

options.text: The condition of the atmosphere at a particular place over a short period of time
The condition of the atmosphere at a particular place over a long period of time
The condition of the atmosphere which occurs in summer
The condition of the atmosphere which occurs in winter

questions.explanation: Weather describes what the atmosphere is like right now or over just a few days – like whether it's hot, rainy, or windy today in Gaborone. When we talk about conditions over a long period of time, like many years, that's called climate, not weather. Think of it this way: weather is what you wear today, while climate helps you decide what clothes to keep in your wardrobe all year round.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: New moon occurs …

options.text: When the whole moon is dark
When half the moon is in shadow
When the bright side is towards the earth

questions.explanation: A new moon happens when the moon is between the Earth and the Sun, so the side of the moon facing us gets no sunlight and appears completely dark in our sky. This is different from when half the moon is in shadow, which we call a quarter moon or half moon. The new moon occurs once every month, not three times, as part of the moon's regular cycle around Earth.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Match: Warm front …

options.text: Where cold air replaces warm air
An area of high air pressure caused by cold air
Where warm air replaces cold air

questions.explanation: A warm front forms when a mass of warm air moves forward and pushes up over colder air that was already in an area, replacing it. You might have been tempted by option A, but that describes a cold front instead, which is the opposite situation where cold air pushes under and replaces warm air. Think of it this way: the word "warm front" tells you that warm air is at the front, leading the movement!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Match: Anti-cyclone sinking …

options.text: Where cold air replaces warm air
An area of high air pressure caused by cold air sinking towards surface
Where warm air replaces cold air

questions.explanation: An anti-cyclone forms when cold, heavy air sinks down towards the Earth's surface, creating an area of high air pressure—just like when you push down on water, the pressure increases at the bottom. Options A and D describe weather fronts, which are different weather features where two different air masses meet and move past each other, not areas of sinking air. Anti-cyclones bring us clear, calm weather because the sinking air stops clouds from forming.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Match: Cold front …

options.text: Where cold air replaces warm air
An area of high air pressure caused by cold air
Where warm air replaces cold air

questions.explanation: A cold front happens when a mass of cold air moves in and pushes the warm air out of the way, replacing it at ground level. This is different from option D because the cold air is doing the replacing, not being replaced - imagine cold air sliding under warm air like a wedge and forcing it upward. While cold air does create high pressure (option B), a cold front specifically describes the boundary where this replacement is happening, not just the high pressure area itself.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following substances is NOT a compound?
questions.explanation: Iron is a pure element made of only one type of atom, which means it cannot be a compound. Compounds like water are made when two or more different elements join together chemically, such as hydrogen and oxygen combining to form water. Remember that elements are the building blocks, while compounds are what you get when different elements bond together!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is a habitat?
questions.explanation: A habitat is the natural home where an organism lives, just like your house is your home. It includes the specific place where a plant or animal naturally lives and finds everything it needs to survive, such as a bird living in a tree or a fish living in a river. While option D might seem close, a habitat is more than just surroundings – it's the actual home itself where the organism belongs.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is used to collect rain water in large quantities?
questions.explanation: A dam is a large structure built across a river or valley that collects and stores huge amounts of rainwater for entire communities, farms, and towns to use. While tanks can also store rainwater, they are much smaller and only hold enough water for one household or building. Dams like the ones you might have seen around Botswana can hold millions of litres of water, making them perfect for collecting rainwater in large quantities.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is represented by letter Z? (LIQUID → Z → SOLID)
questions.explanation: When a substance changes from liquid to solid, it loses heat energy and becomes harder, which is exactly what happens during freezing – think of water turning into ice in your freezer. Melting is the opposite process where a solid turns into a liquid when it gains heat, like ice cream melting in the sun. The arrow Z shows the direction from liquid to solid, so it must represent freezing.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which element is represented by the symbol Fe?
questions.explanation: Iron is represented by the symbol Fe, which comes from its Latin name "ferrum." This is why we see Fe on the Periodic Table instead of just I, which helps avoid confusion with other elements. Lead might seem tempting because it's also a well-known metal, but lead's symbol is actually Pb (from the Latin word "plumbum").

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following forms an element?
questions.explanation: An element is made up of only one type of atom, so when you have the same atoms joined together, you form an element like oxygen or gold. Different atoms joining together would actually form a compound, not an element, because compounds are made when two or more different types of atoms combine.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following symbols is used to show the wind direction in a weather chart?
questions.explanation: Wind direction on weather charts is shown using an arrow symbol that points in the direction the wind is blowing toward, which is what Option C shows. This arrow makes it easy to see at a glance which way the wind is moving across different areas on the map. Some students might confuse this with other weather symbols like those for rain or temperature, but the arrow is specifically designed to show movement and direction of wind.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How can a mixture of pepper and sand be separated?
questions.explanation: To separate pepper and sand, you would first add water to dissolve any soluble materials, then filter the mixture to separate the solid particles, and finally use a magnet to attract and remove any magnetic materials like iron particles that might be in the sand. A permanent magnet alone (option D) wouldn't work because neither pepper nor sand are magnetic materials, so the magnet would not attract them. This method combines three different separation techniques to ensure you can separate all the components effectively.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes the water cycle?
questions.explanation: The water cycle follows a natural order where water from rivers, lakes, and oceans heats up and rises into the sky as water vapor (evaporation), then cools down to form clouds (condensation), and finally falls back to Earth as rain or snow (precipitation). Option A is incorrect because rain must fall from clouds that have already formed, so precipitation cannot happen before evaporation and condensation. This cycle keeps repeating, providing us with the water we need for drinking, farming, and all life in Botswana.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following statements best describes vaccination?

options.text: Method of breeding mosquitoes to produce malaria
Method of giving medicine to cure bacteria
Method of controlling the spread of diseases
Method of protecting people against diseases

questions.explanation: Vaccination is a way to protect your body against diseases before you even get sick by helping your immune system learn to fight off germs. It's different from taking medicine to cure a disease you already have, which is what option C might make you think. Vaccines prevent diseases like measles, polio, and tetanus by training your body to recognize and fight them if you're ever exposed.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens to the melting point of ice when salt is added?

options.text: The melting point does not change
The melting point increases and reduces intervals

questions.explanation: When you add salt to ice, the melting point decreases, which means the ice will melt at a temperature lower than 0°C. This is why we sometimes put salt on icy roads in cold places – the salt helps the ice melt even when the temperature is below freezing. The salt particles get between the water molecules and make it harder for them to form solid ice, so the ice needs to be colder to stay frozen.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a traditional scientific practice used in Botswana?
questions.explanation: Making clay pots is a traditional scientific practice in Botswana because it involves observing nature, understanding which clay works best, and applying knowledge of heat and materials that has been passed down through generations. While beef processing is an important industry in Botswana today, it uses modern technology rather than traditional methods. Car and computer manufacturing are modern industrial practices that are not traditionally done in Botswana.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Who discovered penicillin?
questions.explanation: Alexander Fleming is the scientist who discovered penicillin in 1928 when he noticed that mold killed bacteria in his laboratory. Louis Pasteur was also an important scientist who studied diseases and germs, but he lived earlier and worked on vaccines, not antibiotics like penicillin. This discovery by Fleming was very important because penicillin became the first antibiotic medicine that saved millions of lives from bacterial infections.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is pasteurization?

options.text: Boiling milk at a high temperature
Heating milk to a certain temperature and cooling it immediately
Keeping milk in a warm place
Boiling milk to make it sweet

questions.explanation: Pasteurization is a special process where we heat milk to just the right temperature (not as hot as boiling) and then cool it down quickly to kill harmful germs that could make us sick. This is different from boiling milk at a high temperature, which would make the milk taste different and destroy some of the good nutrients we need. The careful heating and quick cooling keeps milk safe to drink while maintaining its fresh taste and nutritional value.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which term refers to the total number of organisms living in a particular area?
questions.explanation: A population is the total number of organisms of the same species living together in one area, like all the zebras in the Okavango Delta or all the acacia trees in a certain part of the savanna. You might have thought it was a community, but a community includes many different types of organisms living together, not just one type. Remember: population = one species, community = many different species together!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Children learn about wildlife by watching their natural surroundings. What kind of conservation benefit is this?
questions.explanation: When children learn about wildlife by watching animals in their surroundings, they develop knowledge and appreciation that becomes part of their community's way of life and traditions - this is a cultural benefit because it shapes how people understand and value nature as part of their heritage. While this might seem social since it involves people learning together, cultural benefits specifically relate to knowledge, beliefs, and practices that are passed down through generations. This traditional knowledge helps communities protect wildlife because they understand its importance to their identity and history.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which human activity can cause air pollution?
questions.explanation: When we burn fuels like petrol, diesel, coal, or wood, smoke and harmful gases are released into the air, which directly causes air pollution. While cutting down trees (option A) is harmful to the environment because trees help clean the air, it doesn't directly release pollutants into the air the way burning fuels does. Burning is the activity that actually puts dirty smoke and gases into the air we breathe.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Factories produce a lot of unwanted chemicals which are disposed in rivers. Which type of pollution is likely to occur?
questions.explanation: When factories dump unwanted chemicals directly into rivers, those harmful substances mix with and contaminate the water, causing water pollution. This makes the river water unsafe for drinking, bathing, and can harm fish and other water animals. Even though factories might also cause air pollution through their smoke and chimneys, the question specifically tells us the chemicals are being disposed into rivers, which means the pollution happens in the water itself.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How does overpopulation affect people's lives?
questions.explanation: When too many people live in one area, they create more pollution from cars, factories, and burning fuel, which makes the air dirty and harmful to breathe. This polluted air damages our lungs and causes respiratory diseases like asthma and bronchitis, making it harder for people to breathe properly. Overpopulation actually makes people less healthy, not stronger, because crowded conditions also spread diseases more easily and put pressure on hospitals and clean water supplies.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Wendy put salt and sand in two separate containers and added water to both and stirred. Salt disappeared while sand did not. We say salt is …
questions.explanation: When a substance dissolves and disappears in water like the salt did, we call that substance soluble in water. Sand did not dissolve, so we say sand is insoluble in water. While it's true that salt becomes a solute when it dissolves in water, the word "soluble" is the correct term to describe salt's ability to dissolve.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the statement below is not true about hard water?

options.text: Does not lather well with soap

questions.explanation: Hard water contains minerals like calcium and magnesium that make it difficult to form soap lather, so it definitely does not lather well with soap. This means option C is not true about hard water because it says the opposite - that hard water lathers well, which is incorrect. Hard water actually wastes soap because you need to use more of it to get bubbles, and it leaves white chalky deposits (fur) on kettles and pots.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How can hard water cause problem?

options.text: It forms fur inside a kettle
It uses less fuel when boiling

questions.explanation: Hard water contains dissolved minerals like calcium and magnesium, and when you heat this water in a kettle, these minerals settle and form a white, chalky layer called fur or scale on the inside. This fur buildup is a real problem because it makes the kettle less efficient and harder to clean. Option D might seem correct because hard water does actually use more soap (not less) since the minerals react with soap and make it harder to form lather.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following does not help substances to dissolve faster?
questions.explanation: When a substance dissolves, stirring helps mix it faster, heating gives particles more energy to break apart, and smaller particle size means more surface area touches the liquid. Humidity, which is the amount of water vapor in the air around you, does not affect how fast something dissolves in a liquid because dissolving happens inside the solution, not in the air outside it.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens during a lunar eclipse?

options.text: Planet Pluto blocks the sun from reaching the earth
The moon moves into the earth's shadow
The moon passes between the earth and the sun and its shadow falls on earth
Meteorites block the sun from reaching the moon

questions.explanation: During a lunar eclipse, the Earth passes directly between the Sun and the Moon, causing the Moon to move into Earth's shadow and appear dark or reddish in our sky. Option C might sound correct, but that's actually describing a solar eclipse, where the Moon passes between Earth and the Sun, blocking sunlight from reaching parts of Earth. Remember: lunar eclipse means the Moon gets dark, solar eclipse means the Sun gets blocked from our view!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following human activities leads to air pollution?

options.text: Poisonous gases released into the atmosphere
Throwing leftovers and other harmful materials everywhere on land

questions.explanation: Air pollution happens when harmful substances are released into the air we breathe, and poisonous gases from factories, vehicles, and burning materials directly pollute our atmosphere. While throwing waste in water causes water pollution and littering on land causes land pollution, these don't affect the air quality. Remember, each type of pollution affects a different part of our environment - air, water, or land!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which career requires the application of science and technology?
questions.explanation: Mechanical engineering is a career where people design and build machines like cars, engines, and factory equipment by using scientific principles such as forces, energy, and materials, along with technology like computers and tools. While journalism might seem like it uses technology because reporters use phones and cameras, the main work is about gathering and writing news stories, not applying scientific knowledge to solve technical problems. Scientists and engineers use their understanding of how things work in nature to create new inventions and improve existing machines.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens to the people when they drink polluted water?
questions.explanation: When people drink polluted water, harmful germs and chemicals enter their bodies, which makes them become sick with diseases like diarrhea, cholera, or typhoid. Clean water keeps us healthy, while polluted water does the opposite - it cannot make us well or wise, and it definitely does not contain alcohol to make people drunk. This is why it is so important to always drink clean, safe water from protected sources.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a social benefit of conservation in Botswana?

options.text: Planting trees to prevent soil erosion

questions.explanation: When we talk about social benefits, we mean things that bring happiness, enjoyment, and better quality of life to people in our communities. Enjoying viewing wildlife like elephants at Chobe or giraffes in the Okavango Delta is a social benefit because it brings joy to families and visitors, making our lives richer and more meaningful. Employment and tree planting are important benefits of conservation too, but they are economic and environmental benefits rather than social ones.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How has the wheel invention helped in the development of transport system?

options.text: Vehicles are not comfortable as they used to be
Vehicles travel at a very low speed nowadays
Vehicles use a lot of fuel to turn well

questions.explanation: The invention of the wheel made vehicles much more comfortable because wheels allow smooth rolling movement instead of dragging or bumping along rough ground. Before wheels, people had to carry heavy loads on their backs or drag them, which was very uncomfortable and tiring. Wheels also helped vehicles travel faster and use less energy, not more fuel as option C suggests, making transportation easier and more pleasant for everyone.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following water lathers easily?
questions.explanation: Rain water lathers easily with soap because it is soft water, meaning it contains very few dissolved minerals like calcium and magnesium. Borehole water and well water often contain many dissolved minerals from underground rocks, making them hard water that doesn't lather well with soap. Sea water is also very hard because it contains lots of dissolved salts, which is why it's difficult to create soap lather in ocean water.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How does tourism benefit people who live around the Okavango delta?
questions.explanation: Tourism creates many opportunities for people living near the Okavango Delta to earn money by selling crafts, offering guided tours, providing accommodation, and sharing their local knowledge with visitors. This business relationship helps families support themselves while protecting the delta's wildlife and natural beauty. The other options describe harmful activities that would actually chase tourists away and are not the real benefits that local communities gain from tourism.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which organism is a secondary consumer?
questions.context_text: Use the food web to answer questions 49–50.
questions.explanation: A secondary consumer is an animal that eats primary consumers (plant-eaters). The snake is a secondary consumer because it eats the chicken and grasshopper, which are primary consumers that feed on plants. The grasshopper and chicken are primary consumers themselves, while the vulture is a scavenger that feeds on dead animals, making it different from a typical secondary consumer.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which organism is a producer?
questions.explanation: Grass is a producer because it makes its own food using sunlight, water, and air through a process called photosynthesis. Birds, worms, and eagles are all consumers because they cannot make their own food and must eat other living things to survive. Producers like grass are always plants because only plants can trap the sun's energy to create food.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: In a certain habitat they are experiencing drought. What is likely to happen to plants and animals in that area?

options.text: More animals will come to the area

questions.explanation: During a drought, there is not enough water for plants to grow well, and many plants die. When plants die or become scarce, animals don't have enough food and water to survive, so many animals also die or move away to find better conditions, which means the population decreases. The population cannot increase or stay the same because living things need water to survive, and more animals would not come to an area where resources are scarce.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the process through which sugars are converted to alcohol called?
questions.explanation: Fermentation is the special process where tiny living things called yeast break down sugars and change them into alcohol and carbon dioxide gas. This is how drinks like traditional beer are made! Distillation is different – it's a process that happens after fermentation, where liquids are heated to separate alcohol from water to make it stronger.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is meant by the word 'Community'?

options.text: Identical plants that live together in a certain area
Different plants that live together in a certain area
Different plants and animals' that live together in an area

questions.explanation: A community in ecology includes all the living things in an area - both plants AND animals living together, like the acacia trees, grass, zebras, and lions all sharing the savanna. Option A is incorrect because a community must have different types of organisms, not just identical plants, and it must include animals too, not only plants. Think of your village community - it has many different people working together, just like an ecological community has many different species living together!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What contribution did Alexander Graham Bell make in science?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell is famous for inventing the telephone in 1876, which allowed people to speak to each other across long distances for the first time. While Thomas Edison invented the electric light bulb, Bell's telephone revolutionized communication and made it possible for families and businesses around the world to stay connected. His invention was so important that we still use telephones today, though they now look very different from his original design!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When does lunar eclipse occur?

options.text: Only when it is near the moon
During any phase of the moon
Sometimes it occurs during the full moon

questions.explanation: A lunar eclipse happens when the Earth moves between the Sun and the Moon, blocking sunlight from reaching the Moon. This can only occur during a full moon because that's when the Moon is on the opposite side of Earth from the Sun, allowing Earth's shadow to fall on it. While every lunar eclipse needs a full moon, not every full moon creates an eclipse because the Moon's orbit is slightly tilted, so Earth's shadow usually misses the Moon.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which phase of the moon will a person in town X observe?
questions.explanation: When the Moon is on the opposite side of Earth from the Sun, the entire side facing us is lit up brightly, which we call a full moon. A new moon happens when the Moon is between Earth and the Sun, so we cannot see it at all because the lit side faces away from us. By looking at where the Moon is positioned relative to Earth and the Sun in the diagram, you can tell which phase people in town X will observe.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What observation will Maungo make?
questions.context_text: Maungo mixed salt with hot water and stirred. Then he mixed cooking oil with water and stirred.

options.text: Salt dissolves; Cooking oil does not dissolve
Salt does not dissolve; Cooking oil does not dissolve
Salt does not dissolve; Cooking oil dissolves

questions.explanation: When salt mixes with hot water, it breaks down into tiny particles that spread throughout the water and disappear - this is called dissolving. Cooking oil, however, does not dissolve in water because oil and water do not mix together; instead, the oil floats on top or forms separate droplets no matter how much you stir.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: As Lebo was eating her dinner she realized she forgot to add salt. Which type of salt do you recommend her to use?
questions.explanation: When you need salt to dissolve quickly in your food that's already prepared, fine salt works best because its tiny grains have more surface area and mix into your dinner almost immediately. Coarse salt has larger crystals that take much longer to dissolve, so you might end up with crunchy bits of salt in some bites while other parts of your food have no salt at all.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of these substances is formed on posts and water pipes as a result of using hard water?
questions.explanation: When hard water is heated in pipes or kettles, minerals like calcium and magnesium form a hard, crusty layer called fur (also known as limescale). While scum is also caused by hard water, it forms on top of water when soap is mixed with it, not on the pipes themselves. Carbonate and calcium are chemicals that make up fur, but fur is the name we give to the whole deposit that builds up.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: In which water will sugar dissolve faster?
questions.explanation: Sugar dissolves faster in hot water because heat makes water molecules move more quickly, allowing them to break down and mix with the sugar particles more rapidly. Both boiling water (the hottest) and warm water will dissolve sugar much faster than cold water, which is why option D is correct. Option A is tempting, but warm water also dissolves sugar quickly, so we should include both types of hot water in our answer.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a traditional scientific practice in Botswana?
questions.explanation: Drying of mophane worms is a traditional scientific practice because it uses knowledge of the sun and heat to preserve food for later use, which involves understanding how moisture removal prevents spoilage. Making chibuku and processing flour are food preparation methods, but they are not considered scientific practices in the traditional sense. The drying process shows how our ancestors used scientific observation and methods to solve real problems in their daily lives.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Neelo added sugar into water until it could no longer dissolve. The solution is said to be …
questions.explanation: When a solution cannot dissolve any more solute (like sugar) because it has reached its maximum capacity, we call it a saturated solution. An unsaturated solution can still dissolve more sugar, which is the opposite of what happened in Neelo's experiment. Think of it like a sponge that has soaked up all the water it possibly can – it's completely full or "saturated."

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which statement describes a habitat?

options.text: A place where organisms are fed
A place where organisms are killed

questions.explanation: A habitat is the natural home or environment where an organism lives, just like how you live in your house or village. It includes everything the organism needs - food, water, shelter, and space - not just one thing like feeding or reproducing. While organisms do feed and reproduce in their habitats, these are just activities that happen there, not what defines a habitat itself.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the chemical symbol for sodium?
questions.explanation: Sodium is a soft, silvery metal element, and scientists use the chemical symbol Na to represent it, which comes from its Latin name "natrium." You might think the symbol should be "So" because it starts with those letters, but chemical symbols often come from Latin or Greek names, which is why sodium uses Na instead. Another element, potassium, uses the symbol K (from its Latin name "kalium"), so it's important to learn these special symbols rather than just guessing from the English name.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What will happen to people who drink polluted water?
questions.explanation: When people drink polluted water, they become sick because the water contains harmful germs, chemicals, or dirt that can cause diseases like diarrhea, cholera, or typhoid. Clean, safe water is what keeps our bodies healthy and strong, not polluted water which does the opposite by making us ill. This is why it's so important to always drink clean water from safe sources like treated taps or protected wells.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of science?

options.text: Science improves the standard of living
Science helps in the discovery of medicine

questions.explanation: Science actually encourages people to be active, curious, and hardworking as they explore, experiment, and discover new things. The other options are all true benefits of science – it helps us solve everyday problems, improves our quality of life through better technology and resources, and has led to discovering medicines that save millions of lives. Being lazy is the opposite of what science promotes, as scientists must be dedicated and persistent in their work.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes an ecosystem?

options.text: All living organisms in an area
Living and non-living things interacting in an area

questions.explanation: An ecosystem includes both the living things like plants, animals, and insects, AND the non-living things like water, soil, air, and sunlight that all work together in an area. Think of the Okavango Delta - it's not just the elephants and fish living there, but also the water, sand, and sunshine that make it a complete ecosystem. Option A might seem correct, but it only mentions living organisms and forgets the important non-living parts like water and soil that living things need to survive.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a producer in the food chain?
questions.explanation: Grass is a producer because it makes its own food using sunlight, water, and air through photosynthesis. Animals like zebras, lions, and eagles are all consumers because they must eat other living things to get energy - they cannot make their own food like plants do. Producers are always plants or plant-like organisms that start the food chain by capturing energy from the sun.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is meant by the term 'population'?

options.text: A group of organisms of the same species living in an area

questions.explanation: A population is a group of the same type of organism living together in one place, like a group of impalas in the Okavango Delta or all the mopane trees in one area. Remember that a population must be the same species - if you count all the different animals together, that would be a community, not a population. This is why option A is incorrect, as it mixes different animal species together instead of focusing on just one type.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a correct food chain?
questions.explanation: A food chain shows how energy flows from plants to animals, always starting with a producer like grass that makes its own food from sunlight, then moving through consumers from smallest to largest. Option B is correct because it follows this natural order: grass feeds insects, insects are eaten by frogs, frogs are eaten by snakes, and snakes are eaten by hawks. Option A is backwards - hawks cannot give energy to snakes, and animals cannot give energy back to grass!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following are correctly classified as elements and compounds?
questions.explanation: An element is a pure substance made of only one type of atom, like zinc or oxygen, while a compound is made of two or more different elements chemically joined together, like water which contains hydrogen and oxygen atoms bonded together. Zinc is correctly identified as an element because it contains only zinc atoms, and water is a compound because it's made from hydrogen and oxygen combined. Remember that oxygen and hydrogen by themselves are both elements, not compounds, so option B incorrectly labels hydrogen as a compound when it's actually an element on its own.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What kind of energy conversion is taking place from the above diagram?
questions.explanation: When a candle burns, the wax (which is a chemical) breaks down and releases energy that we can see as light from the flame and feel as heat warming the air around it. This is why the answer is chemical to light and heat energy. Option C might seem correct because we often see electrical devices producing light, but a candle doesn't use electricity at all – it uses the stored chemical energy in the wax itself.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which pair of substances is soluble in water?
questions.explanation: When you stir salt and sugar into water, they both dissolve completely and form a clear solution, making them soluble substances. Flour and mealier-meal might seem to mix with water at first, but they actually just float around and settle at the bottom instead of dissolving - try this at home and you'll see the water stays cloudy! Sand never dissolves in water either, so even though sugar does, sand and sugar together aren't both soluble.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Heat from the sun travels through the air and directly reaches us. This method of heat transfer is called …
questions.explanation: Heat from the sun travels through empty space and air without needing any material to carry it – this special way of heat traveling is called radiation, just like how sunlight warms your skin even though you're not touching the sun. Convection is different because it needs air or liquid to move around in circles to carry the heat, like when hot air rises in a room. Radiation is the only way heat can travel through space where there is no air at all, which is why the sun's warmth can reach Earth.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What would you expect the size of the image formed by a plane mirror to be?
questions.explanation: When you look in a plane mirror, like the one in your bathroom, the image you see is always exactly the same size as you are. The mirror doesn't make things smaller like when you step back from something, or bigger like a magnifying glass does – it simply reflects light straight back to show things as they really are. This is why you can use a mirror to check if your school uniform fits properly, because the reflection shows your true size!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following shows refraction of light?
questions.explanation: Refraction happens when light bends as it passes from one material into another, like when a straw looks bent in a glass of water or when light passes through a prism. Option B correctly shows this bending of light as it moves between different materials. Remember that reflection (bouncing off a surface like a mirror) is different from refraction, so don't confuse the two!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: When a red litmus paper is put in sugary liquid, it does not change its colour. This shows that the liquid is …

options.text: It is an acid and alkali

questions.explanation: When red litmus paper stays red and doesn't change color, it means the liquid is neither acidic nor alkaline - it is neutral. If the liquid were an alkali (option A), the red litmus paper would have turned blue, but since it stayed red, we know the sugary liquid has a neutral pH, just like pure water.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which acid is strong?
questions.explanation: Sulphuric acid is a strong acid because it completely breaks apart in water and can cause serious burns or damage to materials very quickly.
