The other acids listed - carbonic acid (found in fizzy drinks), citric acid (found in lemons), and tartaric acid (found in grapes) - are all weak acids that only partially break apart in water, which is why you can safely eat or drink things containing them.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The following are ways of reducing friction EXCEPT …
questions.explanation: Friction happens when surfaces rub against each other, and rough surfaces create more friction than smooth ones. Streamlining, ball bearings, and oiling all make surfaces smoother or reduce contact, which decreases friction. Increasing roughness does the opposite – it makes surfaces grip more and creates even more friction, just like how rough sandpaper grips better than smooth glass.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which letter represents the load?
questions.explanation: In a lever system, the load is the object or weight that needs to be moved or lifted. B is correct because it represents the load - the heavy object being lifted by the lever. A represents the fulcrum (the pivot point), while C shows the effort (the force you apply), and D is just part of the lever arm itself.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following does not increase the risk of lightning strikes?

options.text: Using a lightning conductor in buildings

questions.explanation: A lightning conductor is a safety device that actually protects buildings by safely directing lightning strikes down to the ground, so it reduces the risk rather than increasing it. The other options all increase your danger because water, tall objects, and metal wires can attract lightning or help it travel to you during a thunderstorm.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Why are parallel circuits mostly preferred?

options.text: If there is a break in one current flow, other current flow paths are affected
If there is a break in one current flow, other current flow paths are not affected
It is easier and cheap to afford

questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, electricity has multiple paths to flow through, so if one bulb or device breaks, the others keep working because they have their own separate paths. This is why our homes use parallel circuits - when one light bulb stops working, the other lights in your house stay on! Option A is incorrect because it says the opposite - in parallel circuits, a break in one path does NOT affect the other paths, which is exactly what makes them so useful.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which type of force causes friction?
questions.explanation: Friction happens when two surfaces rub against each other, like when you push a book across your desk or when your shoes grip the ground as you walk. This rubbing between touching surfaces is a contact force, which means the objects must be physically touching for friction to occur. While gravity pulls objects down and can press them together (which affects how strong friction is), gravity itself does not create the friction - it's the contact between the surfaces that does.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is a lever?
questions.explanation: A lever is a simple machine because it has just one or two parts that work together to make our work easier, like a seesaw or a bottle opener. Simple machines like levers use basic movements to help us lift or move heavy objects with less effort. They are not complex machines because complex machines have many moving parts working together, like a bicycle or a car.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: In which class of lever is the pivot between the load and the effort?
questions.explanation: In a first class lever, the pivot (fulcrum) is always positioned between the load and the effort, just like a seesaw where the middle support is between the two people. Second class levers have the load in the middle (like a wheelbarrow), while third class levers have the effort in the middle (like fishing rods or tweezers). Remember the order: First class = Fulcrum in middle, Second class = Load in middle, Third class = Effort in middle.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a source of static electricity?
questions.explanation: When you rub two objects together, like when you drag your feet across a carpet or comb your hair, electrons move from one object to the other and create static electricity - that's friction at work! Batteries, generators, and solar panels all produce current electricity that flows through wires, which is different from static electricity that builds up and stays in one place until it suddenly jumps (like a shock). Friction is the only way listed here that creates that buildup of electrical charge we call static electricity.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: At night pedestrians should wear white clothing so that …

options.text: They can clearly see where they are going

questions.explanation: When light from car headlights shines on white clothing at night, the white material reflects the light back strongly, making the pedestrian easy to spot from far away. This helps drivers see pedestrians in time to slow down or stop safely. Dark clothing would absorb the light instead of reflecting it, making it very difficult for drivers to notice people walking along the road.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which organ of the digestive system is correctly matched with its function?
questions.explanation: The liver is a very important organ that produces bile, a greenish liquid that helps break down fats in your food so your body can use them. Many students confuse the oesophagus with the intestines, but the oesophagus is actually just a muscular tube that pushes food down to your stomach – it's the large intestine that absorbs water from undigested food. Remember that each organ in your digestive system has its own special job to keep you healthy and strong!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The part labeled a is …
questions.explanation: The ovary is the female reproductive organ that produces and stores eggs, so when you see a diagram showing the larger rounded structure where eggs develop, that's the ovary. Students sometimes confuse this with the egg itself, but remember that the egg is much smaller and is actually produced inside the ovary before being released. Think of the ovary as a factory that makes eggs, not the egg itself!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the function of uterus?
questions.explanation: The uterus is a special organ in a woman's body that acts like a safe, comfortable home where a baby grows and develops for about 9 months during pregnancy. While the vagina does receive sperms during reproduction (option A), this is not the uterus's main job – the uterus's most important function is to protect and nourish the growing baby until it is ready to be born. The ovaries, not the uterus, are responsible for producing ova (eggs).

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which planet is closest to the sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the smallest planet in our solar system and sits right next to the Sun as the first planet. Venus might seem like a good choice because it's the hottest planet, but it's actually the second planet from the Sun, with Mercury being even closer. If you remember the order of planets starting from the Sun, it goes: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, and then the outer planets.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What keeps the planets in their orbits around the sun?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the invisible force of attraction between the Sun and the planets that pulls them toward the Sun, keeping them moving in curved paths called orbits instead of flying off into space. Magnetism might seem like a good answer because magnets also pull objects, but planets aren't magnets and magnetic forces only work on certain metals, not on all the rocks and gases that make up planets. Friction and wind cannot work in space because there is no air in the empty space between planets.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following describes a solar eclipse?

options.text: Moon passes between earth and sun
Earth passes between moon and sun
Sun passes between earth and moon

questions.explanation: A solar eclipse happens when the Moon moves directly between the Earth and the Sun, blocking the Sun's light and casting a shadow on Earth. Option B describes a lunar eclipse instead, which is when Earth blocks sunlight from reaching the Moon. The Sun is far too large and always stays in the center of our solar system, so it cannot pass between Earth and the Moon.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the order of the planets from the sun?

options.text: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
Venus, Mercury, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
Mercury, Earth, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
Mercury, Venus, Mars, Earth, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune

questions.explanation: The planets orbit the Sun in this order from closest to farthest: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun because it has the smallest orbit, while Neptune is the farthest away. A helpful way to remember this order is to create a sentence where each word starts with the same letter as each planet in order!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a way to conserve water?
questions.explanation: Fixing leaking pipes is an excellent way to conserve water because even a small leak can waste hundreds of litres of clean water every day that could be used by families and farms. When we leave taps running or use more water than we need, we are actually wasting water instead of conserving it. In Botswana where water is precious, every drop we save by fixing leaks helps ensure there is enough water for everyone.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What does a barometer measure?
questions.explanation: A barometer is a special instrument that measures air pressure, which is the weight of all the air pushing down on us from above. When air pressure changes, it helps us predict if the weather will be sunny, rainy, or stormy. While a thermometer measures temperature and other instruments measure wind speed or humidity, a barometer specifically tells us about the pressure of the air around us.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which statement is correct about the speed of sound?

options.text: Sound travels faster in gas than in liquid
Sound travels faster in gas than in solids
Sound travels faster in liquid than in solids
Sound travels faster in solids than in gas

questions.explanation: Sound waves travel fastest through solids because the particles are packed very close together, allowing vibrations to pass quickly from one particle to the next. In gases like air, the particles are far apart, so sound moves much more slowly because each vibration takes longer to reach the next particle. This is why you can hear a train coming from far away if you put your ear to the railway track, but it takes longer to hear it through the air.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following shows a symbol of a closed switch?
questions.explanation: A closed switch in a circuit diagram is shown by two lines that touch each other, allowing electricity to flow through without any gap. Option B shows an open switch where the lines don't meet, which would stop the electricity from flowing. Remember, a closed switch is like a closed door that completes the path for electricity to travel around the circuit.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which problem is associated with backstreet abortion?

options.text: The mother may lose a lot of blood
They will be high birth rate
She may be fertile for the rest of her life

questions.explanation: Backstreet abortions are very dangerous because they are done in unsafe places without proper medical care or clean equipment, which can cause serious bleeding and infections that put the mother's life at risk. This is completely different from proper medical care, which aims to keep people healthy and safe. The other options describe positive health outcomes, but backstreet abortions actually cause serious harm to a woman's health and can even lead to death.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the difference between hard and soft water?

options.text: Hard water lathers easily while soft water does not
Soft water contains dissolved minerals while hard water does not
Hard water boils more quickly while soft water does not
Soft water lathers easily while hard water does not

questions.explanation: When you add soap to soft water, it creates lots of bubbles and lathers easily, making it great for washing. Hard water contains dissolved minerals like calcium and magnesium that prevent soap from lathering well, so you need to use more soap to get bubbles. Remember, hard water has the minerals, not soft water, which is why option B is the opposite of what is true.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following methods can be used to separate a mixture of sand and iron fillings?
questions.explanation: Iron is magnetic, which means it is attracted to magnets, while sand is not magnetic at all. This means you can use a magnet to pull out all the iron filings from the mixture, leaving the sand behind. Filtration would not work here because both sand and iron filings are solid particles that would stay together on the filter paper.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a weak alkali?
questions.explanation: Sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) is a weak alkali because it only releases a small amount of hydroxide ions when dissolved in water, making it gentle and safe to use in cooking and cleaning. The other options - calcium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, and sodium hydroxide - are all strong alkalis that release many hydroxide ions and can be dangerous to touch or swallow because they are very corrosive. You can tell sodium bicarbonate is weaker because it has a pH closer to 7, while strong alkalis have pH values much higher, around 12-14.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: All the following diseases are immunized against in Botswana except …
questions.explanation: In Botswana's national immunization program, children receive vaccines to protect against tetanus, whooping cough, and diphtheria as part of routine childhood vaccinations. Typhoid fever is not included in the standard immunization schedule, though it may be given separately to people traveling to high-risk areas. The vaccines for tetanus, whooping cough, and diphtheria are often given together in one injection called the DPT vaccine.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: It is advisable to watch the solar eclipse with …
questions.explanation: Looking directly at a solar eclipse can seriously damage your eyes because the sun's rays are extremely powerful, even when partially covered by the moon. Specially made eclipse goggles have very strong filters that block out harmful rays and protect your eyes safely. Ordinary sunglasses might seem like they would work, but they are not strong enough to protect your eyes from the sun's dangerous radiation during an eclipse.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How is tuberculosis transmitted?
questions.explanation: Tuberculosis (TB) is spread through the air when a person with TB coughs, sneezes, or talks, releasing tiny droplets that others can breathe in. This is why TB spreads easily in crowded places and why people with TB should cover their mouths when coughing. Unlike diseases such as malaria, TB is not spread by mosquitoes or through water, and it is not a sexually transmitted infection.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Cow eats grass to live and grow. The cow is described as …
questions.explanation: A cow is a consumer because it must eat other living things (like grass) to get energy and grow - it cannot make its own food. Producers are organisms like grass and trees that make their own food using sunlight, water, and air. Since the cow eats the grass rather than making food itself, it is definitely a consumer in the food chain.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is not a type of lever?
questions.explanation: Levers are grouped into three types based on where the fulcrum, load, and effort are positioned: first class, second class, and third class levers. The fulcrum is actually the pivot point that all levers use, not a type of lever itself. Think of it this way: the fulcrum is like the hinge on a door - it's a part that helps the lever work, but it's not a category of lever.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which was the highest cause of road accident?
questions.explanation: Unlicensed drivers cause the most road accidents because they haven't received proper training on road rules, safety procedures, and how to handle different driving situations. While drunken driving is also very dangerous and causes many accidents, statistics show that people driving without a license cause even more accidents because they simply don't know how to drive safely. Bad roads and animals can contribute to accidents, but the driver's skills and training matter most in preventing crashes.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How many accidents were caused by drunken and driving?
questions.explanation: Looking at the data carefully, when we count all the accidents that were caused by drunken driving, we get a total of 75 accidents. You might be tempted to choose 70 if you missed counting some of the accidents or made a small adding mistake, but when you add up all the numbers properly, you will see that 75 is the correct total.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which test tube will the salt dissolve faster in?
questions.context_text: Kato poured same amount of water into test tubes A and B. He added fine salt to A and coarse salt to B, then shook both.

options.text: Both dissolve at the same rate

questions.explanation: Fine salt dissolves faster than coarse salt because it has smaller particles with more surface area touching the water at once. When you shake both test tubes the same way, the fine salt in test tube A will disappear into the water much quicker than the larger coarse salt pieces in test tube B. Think of it like sugar - fine sugar dissolves faster in your tea than big sugar crystals would!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a secondary consumer in the food web?
questions.explanation: A secondary consumer is an animal that eats other animals that have already eaten plants. The snake is a secondary consumer because it eats animals like frogs or mice, which themselves ate insects or plants. A grasshopper is a primary consumer because it only eats plants, and a frog is also a primary consumer when it eats insects that feed on plants.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT used to separate mixtures?
questions.explanation: Combustion is a chemical reaction where substances burn with oxygen to produce new substances like ash and smoke, so it changes materials rather than separating them. Filtration, magnetism, and evaporation are all separation methods that physically separate different parts of a mixture without changing what the substances are made of—for example, you can use a magnet to separate iron filings from sand, or filter to separate water from soil.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Hard water can be softened by …
questions.explanation: When you boil hard water, the heat causes some of the dissolved minerals (like calcium) to form solid deposits that settle at the bottom or stick to the pot, making the water softer. Cooling or freezing the water doesn't change the minerals dissolved in it - they stay mixed in the water whether it's hot or cold. Evaporating would remove the water and leave all the minerals behind, which is the opposite of what we want!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens at a cold front?

options.text: Cold air sinks and rises up

questions.explanation: At a cold front, a mass of cold air moves in and pushes underneath the warm air that was there before, causing the cold air to replace the warm air at ground level. While it's true that warm air does rise when this happens (option C), that's just part of what occurs – the main thing that defines a cold front is that cold air is taking the place of warm air. Think of it like when a cool breeze comes through your window and pushes out the warm air in your room!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Tiroyaone and his friends went to the park for the game viewing. What type of tourism benefit is stated above?
questions.explanation: When Tiroyaone and his friends visit the park together for game viewing, they are enjoying time with each other and having fun as a group, which is a social benefit of tourism. Social benefits are about people spending quality time together and building friendships through activities. While tourism can bring money to an area (economical benefit), this example focuses on the friendship and enjoyment the group experiences together.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which state of the matter is shown in the diagram below?
questions.explanation: Looking at the diagram, you can see particles that are spread far apart and moving randomly in all directions, which is exactly how gas particles behave. Solids might seem tempting because we can see individual particles, but in solids the particles would be tightly packed together in a fixed pattern, not spread out like this. The wide spacing and freedom of movement shown here is the key feature that tells us this is a gas.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a benefit for family planning?

options.text: Inability to care for the baby

questions.explanation: Family planning helps people decide when to have children, allowing them to first finish their education and achieve their goals like completing their studies. When people can plan their families properly, they avoid negative consequences like being unable to care for a baby or having to leave school early. This is why completion of studies is a benefit of family planning, while the other options are problems that family planning helps to prevent.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the relationship between the sun and the earth?

options.text: The sun revolves around the earth
The earth revolves around the sun
The sun and earth revolve around the moon

questions.explanation: The Earth travels around the Sun in a path called an orbit, taking one full year to complete this journey. Long ago, people thought the Sun moved around the Earth because it appears to rise and set each day, but scientists discovered that it's actually our Earth that is spinning and moving around the much larger Sun. The Sun stays at the center of our solar system while all the planets, including Earth, revolve around it.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a source of light?
questions.explanation: The Sun is a source of light because it produces its own light through burning gases, just like a candle flame or light bulb makes light. The Moon might seem like it glows, but it actually just reflects light from the Sun like a mirror does—neither the Moon nor a mirror can make their own light. Windows and mirrors only allow light to pass through or bounce off them, but they cannot create light themselves.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the name given to a mixture that cannot be separated by filtering?
questions.explanation: A solution is a special mixture where one substance dissolves completely into another, like sugar in water, so the particles are too tiny to be trapped by a filter. Suspensions, on the other hand, have larger particles that eventually settle at the bottom and can be separated by filtering, like muddy water. Once a mixture forms a solution, the only way to separate it is through other methods like evaporation or distillation.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: How does a lunar eclipse occur?

options.text: When the moon comes in between the sun and the earth
When the earth comes in between the sun and the moon
When the sun begins to shine backwards
When the moon, earth and the sun are not in a straight line

questions.explanation: A lunar eclipse happens when the Earth moves directly between the Sun and the Moon, blocking sunlight from reaching the Moon's surface. This causes the Moon to darken or turn reddish because it sits in Earth's shadow. Option A describes a solar eclipse instead, which is when the Moon blocks the Sun's light from reaching Earth.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The warm front as a weather system describes a situation whereby …

options.text: The cold air takes the place of the warm air causing rain
The warm air takes the place of the cold air bringing about humidity
Both the warm and cold air produces darkness of clouds
The cold air begins to fight the warm air in the environment

questions.explanation: A warm front happens when warm air moves forward and slides over the cold air that was there before, bringing moisture and humidity to the area. You might think cold air replaces warm air (option A), but that would actually be a cold front, which is the opposite situation. The key is to remember that a warm front means the warm air is advancing and taking over the space where cold air used to be.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a traditional scientific practice in Botswana?

options.text: Burying of the dead after a year
Shouting at midnight during the month of March
Putting on heavy clothes during summer

questions.explanation: Drying mophane worms is a traditional scientific practice because it uses the sun's heat to preserve food by removing water, which stops bacteria from spoiling the worms. This method of food preservation has been used by Batswana for many generations and follows scientific principles of dehydration. The other options are either cultural practices without scientific basis or don't make practical sense, like wearing heavy clothes when it's hot.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following food chains represents the feeding relationship?
questions.context_text: The lion feeds on a hippo while the hippo feeds on grass.
questions.explanation: In a food chain, the arrow shows the direction energy flows, which means it points from what is eaten to what eats it. Since grass is eaten by the hippo, and the hippo is eaten by the lion, the arrows must go Grass → Hippo → Lion. Option A might seem tempting because it lists the animals in the story order, but remember the arrows must always point from the food to the feeder, starting with plants that make their own food.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What will happen to people who drink polluted water?
questions.explanation: Polluted water contains harmful germs, chemicals, and dirt that can make people very sick with diseases like cholera, diarrhea, and typhoid fever. When these dangerous things enter our bodies through drinking, they attack our health instead of helping us stay strong. This is why we must always drink clean, safe water from protected sources like treated taps or boiled water.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a correct food chain?
questions.explanation: A food chain must show how energy flows from the sun to plants, then to animals that eat plants, and finally to animals that eat other animals. Option A correctly shows the sun giving energy to grass (producer), which is eaten by the grasshopper (herbivore), which is then eaten by the frog (carnivore). Option B has the chain backwards—energy cannot flow from animals back to the sun!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following organisms is a decomposer?
questions.explanation: Bacteria are decomposers because they break down dead plants and animals into simpler substances that return nutrients to the soil. Lions and zebras are consumers that eat other living things, while grass is a producer that makes its own food through photosynthesis. Decomposers like bacteria play a very important role in nature by recycling nutrients so that new plants can grow.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is a community in ecology?

options.text: A group of plants of the same species
A group of animals of the same species
Different species of plants and animals living together in an area
Only the producers in an area

questions.explanation: A community in ecology is made up of all the different types of living things sharing the same place, like having different plants, animals, insects, and birds all living together in a forest or savanna. When you see only one type of animal or plant together, like a herd of zebras or a group of mopane trees, that's called a population, not a community. A true community needs many different species interacting with each other in the same area.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a renewable energy source?
questions.explanation: Solar energy is renewable because the sun keeps shining every day and will never run out in our lifetime, so we can use it over and over again. Coal, natural gas, and petroleum are non-renewable because once we burn them, they are gone forever, and it takes millions of years for the Earth to make more of them. We should use more renewable energy sources like solar power to protect our environment and save resources for the future.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What energy changes are taking place in the diagram below?

options.text: Chemical energy to sound + light energy
Chemical energy to heat + light energy

questions.explanation: When a candle burns, the wax (which is a chemical) changes into heat energy that you can feel and light energy that helps you see in the dark. This is why we say chemical energy is converted to heat and light energy. Option B might seem correct because candles can make a crackling sound sometimes, but the main energy change in a burning candle is always chemical energy turning into heat and light, not sound.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Where is the effort applied on the following lever?
questions.explanation: When using a lever, the effort is the force you apply to make the lever work, and looking at the diagram, you can see that point A is where the person pushes down to lift the load. Point B might seem like a good choice because it's in the middle, but that's actually the fulcrum (the pivot point where the lever turns), not where you apply your pushing force. The effort always comes from where you put in your own force to move something.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which one of the following can increase the risk of a lightning strike?
questions.explanation: Lightning looks for the easiest path to reach the ground, so it usually strikes the tallest objects first because they are closest to the clouds where lightning forms. Tall towers are at high risk because they stick up much higher than short buildings, flat ground, or grass, making them like a bridge that brings the lightning closer to Earth.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Vinegar can be classified as …
questions.explanation: Vinegar is a weak acid because it contains acetic acid, which only releases some of its hydrogen ions when mixed with water, making it acidic but not very strong. You can safely use vinegar in cooking and cleaning because it's much milder than strong acids like battery acid, which can cause serious burns. If vinegar were neutral like water, it wouldn't have that sour taste or be able to clean things the way it does!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which circuit connection is best to be used in our homes?
questions.explanation: In our homes, we use parallel circuits because each electrical device (like lights and TV) can work independently - if one bulb breaks, the others keep working, and each device gets the full voltage it needs. A series circuit would not work well because if one device stops working, everything else would also stop working, just like when one bulb breaks in some old Christmas lights, they all go off. Parallel circuits also let us control each device separately with its own switch, which is very practical for everyday use.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the speed of light?
questions.explanation: Light travels incredibly fast at 300,000 kilometers per second, which means it could go around the Earth more than 7 times in just one second! This is much faster than the other options – for example, 300 km/s would be too slow for light to reach us from the Sun in only 8 minutes like it actually does. Remember this speed by thinking "3" with five zeros after it: 300,000 km/s.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a benefit of friction?

options.text: It allows us to walk without slipping

questions.explanation: Friction is actually very helpful because it creates a grip between our feet and the ground, which stops us from slipping when we walk or run. While it's true that friction does slow down vehicles and wear out shoes, these are problems caused by friction, not benefits. A benefit is something good that helps us, and without friction, we wouldn't be able to walk at all – we would just slide around like on ice!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the function of a switch in an electric circuit?

options.text: To open or close the circuit

questions.explanation: A switch works like a gate in an electric circuit - when you press it ON, it closes the circuit and allows electricity to flow through, making your bulb light up or your device work. When you press it OFF, it opens the circuit and stops the electricity from flowing. The switch does not make electricity (that's what a battery or generator does) or store it - it simply controls whether the electricity can flow or not.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which substances are correctly classified as soluble and insoluble?
questions.explanation: When you put Eno in water, it dissolves completely and disappears into the liquid, making it soluble, while sand does not dissolve and settles at the bottom, making it insoluble. Some students might think salt is insoluble, but if you stir salt in water, it dissolves completely just like sugar does – both are soluble substances that disappear into water. Maize meal and flour may seem to mix with water, but they don't actually dissolve; they just float around and eventually settle or form a cloudy mixture.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is true about hard and soft water?

options.text: Hard water shows foam; Soft water shows no foam
Hard water does not form scum; Soft water forms scum
Hard water does not contain dissolved salts; Soft water contains dissolved salts
Hard water does not lather well; Soft water lathers well

questions.explanation: Hard water contains dissolved mineral salts like calcium and magnesium, which prevent soap from forming a good lather and instead create scum or foam on the surface. Soft water, on the other hand, has few or no dissolved salts, so soap easily creates lots of rich lather when you wash with it. This is why soft water is better for washing clothes and bathing, as you need less soap to get things clean!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following animals is NOT protected?
questions.explanation: Jackals are not protected animals in Botswana because they are common and not at risk of disappearing. Animals like cheetahs, vultures, and giraffes are protected by law because their numbers are low and they need special care to survive. Protected animals cannot be hunted or harmed, but jackals can be controlled when they cause problems for farmers.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following describes weather?

options.text: The condition of the atmosphere at a particular place over a short period of time
The condition of the atmosphere at a particular place over a long period of time
The condition of the atmosphere which occurs in summer
The condition of the atmosphere which occurs in winter

questions.explanation: Weather describes what the atmosphere is like right now or over just a few days – like when you check if it's sunny, rainy, or windy today before going to school. Option B describes climate, not weather, because climate is the pattern of atmospheric conditions over many years, such as knowing that Botswana is generally hot and dry. Remember: weather changes quickly (day to day), while climate is the long-term average!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a method of water treatment?
questions.explanation: Sedimentation is a water treatment method where heavy dirt particles and solid materials sink to the bottom of a container, leaving cleaner water on top. This process is commonly used at water treatment plants and even in our homes when we let muddy water settle. Magnetism, sublimation, and combustion are not used for cleaning water because they involve attracting metals, changing solids directly to gas, and burning materials respectively.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the difference between evaporation and boiling?

options.text: Evaporation occurs at a fixed temperature; boiling occurs at any temperature
Evaporation occurs at any temperature; boiling occurs at a fixed temperature
Both occur at the same temperature

questions.explanation: Evaporation can happen at any temperature - you can see wet clothes drying even on a cool day - while boiling only happens when water reaches exactly 100°C. This is why a puddle slowly disappears in the sun without bubbling, but water in a pot must get very hot before it starts to boil with bubbles. Though boiling looks faster because of all the bubbles, evaporation is actually always happening whenever there is liquid water exposed to air.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Copper wire is a conductor of electricity because it allows electric current to flow through it easily, which is why we use copper in electrical wires at home and in appliances. Materials like plastic, rubber, and wood are insulators that block electricity from flowing through them – that's why electrical wires are covered with plastic or rubber coating to keep us safe from electric shocks. Metals like copper are excellent conductors, while most non-metals are insulators.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens to the density of water as it freezes?
questions.explanation: When water freezes into ice, the water molecules arrange themselves into a special pattern with more space between them, making ice less dense than liquid water. This is why ice floats on top of water in your drink or in a pond! Many students think density increases (option A) because ice feels solid and hard, but remember that density is about how tightly packed the molecules are, not how hard something feels.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following describes a lunar eclipse?

options.text: When the moon passes between the earth and the sun
When the earth passes between the sun and the moon
When the sun is directly behind the earth
When planets align with the sun

questions.explanation: A lunar eclipse happens when the Earth moves directly between the Sun and the Moon, blocking sunlight from reaching the Moon's surface. You might have confused this with option A, which actually describes a solar eclipse—that's when the Moon passes between the Earth and the Sun, blocking our view of the Sun. Remember: lunar eclipse = Earth in the middle, solar eclipse = Moon in the middle!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is pasteurization?

options.text: Boiling milk at a high temperature
Heating milk to a certain temperature and cooling it immediately
Keeping milk in a warm place
Boiling milk to make it sweet

questions.explanation: Pasteurization is a special process where we heat milk to a specific temperature (not boiling!) and then cool it down quickly to kill harmful germs that could make us sick. This is different from boiling milk at a high temperature because pasteurization uses a lower, carefully controlled temperature that kills the germs but keeps the milk's good taste and nutrients. The quick heating and cooling makes milk safe to drink while keeping it fresh and healthy for you!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which career does NOT require the application of science and technology?
questions.explanation: An economist studies how people use money and resources in business and government, which involves mathematics but not science and technology in the same way other careers do. A pharmacist must understand chemistry to prepare medicines, a mechanic needs to know how engines and machines work using physics, and an engineer applies scientific principles to design and build structures or systems. While economics uses some mathematical tools, it focuses on human behavior and financial systems rather than applying scientific knowledge about the natural world.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following animal is likely to be found in water?
questions.explanation: Whales are mammals that live their entire lives in water, with special body features like flippers and a streamlined shape that help them swim in oceans and seas. While birds like ducks can swim on water's surface, they don't live in water like whales do - they return to land to rest and build nests. Cats and ants are land animals that would struggle to survive in water because they lack the special adaptations needed for aquatic life.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: A certain habitat experiences drought. What is likely to happen to plants and animals in that area?

options.text: More rainfall will come to the area

questions.explanation: During a drought, there is very little water available, which means plants cannot grow well and many will die. When plants die, animals lose their food and water sources, so many animals will also die or move away to find better conditions, causing the population to decrease. More rainfall (option D) is about weather changes, not about what happens to living things during the drought itself.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a common benefit of conservation to Botswana?

options.text: Earning money from working in game reserves
Protecting plants and animals for future generation

questions.explanation: Conservation means protecting our natural environment so that future generations can also enjoy Botswana's amazing wildlife and plants, like elephants, lions, and mopane trees. While people can earn money by working in game reserves, this is just one job opportunity, not the main reason we protect nature—conservation is really about making sure these precious plants and animals don't disappear forever. By conserving today, we ensure that your children and grandchildren will still be able to see the beautiful wildlife that makes Botswana special.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What weather condition does the symbol below represent?
questions.explanation: When you see a weather symbol with a bright sun and lines coming off to the side, those lines represent wind movement, showing us it will be sunny and windy. Some students might think option B (just sunny) is correct because they notice the sun first, but remember that the extra lines are very important - they always mean wind is present in the forecast. Weather symbols combine different elements to give us complete information about what to expect outside.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The wind vane is an instrument that is used to …

options.text: Show the direction of the wind
Tell the time the wind is going to stop blowing
To tell the speed of the wind

questions.explanation: A wind vane is a simple tool with an arrow that spins freely on top of a pole, and it always points in the direction where the wind is coming from. While it shows wind direction perfectly, it cannot measure how fast the wind is blowing – that's what an anemometer is for! The wind vane just rotates to follow the wind's direction, but it doesn't change or control which way the wind actually blows.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the habitat is NOT found in Botswana?
questions.explanation: Botswana is a landlocked country, which means it is completely surrounded by other countries and has no coastline where it touches the sea. This is why we do not have ocean habitats in Botswana, even though we have desert areas like the Kalahari, rivers like the Okavango, and swamps in the Okavango Delta. To see an ocean, people from Botswana would need to travel to neighbouring countries like Namibia or South Africa.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which human activity can cause air pollution?
questions.explanation: When fuels like petrol, diesel, coal, or wood are burned, they release smoke and harmful gases into the air, which is what we call air pollution. While cutting down trees is harmful to the environment because trees clean the air by absorbing carbon dioxide, it doesn't directly release pollutants into the air the way burning does. Burning fuels in vehicles, factories, and cooking fires is one of the main causes of dirty air in our cities and towns.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the name given to animals that eat both plants and animals?
questions.explanation: Animals that eat both plants and animals are called omnivores because "omni" means "all" - they eat all types of food. You might have been tempted to choose herbivores or carnivores, but remember that herbivores only eat plants (like goats and zebras) and carnivores only eat meat (like lions and leopards). Omnivores like baboons, chickens, and even humans eat a mixture of both plants and animals!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is the gas we breathe in to stay alive, and it makes up about 21% of our atmosphere, but it does not trap heat like greenhouse gases do. Greenhouse gases like carbon dioxide, methane, and water vapour act like a blanket around Earth, trapping the sun's heat and making our planet warmer. Oxygen simply passes heat through without trapping it, which is why it is not considered a greenhouse gas.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a method of conserving water?

options.text: Using water for washing cars daily

questions.explanation: Fixing leaking taps is an excellent way to conserve water because even small drips can waste hundreds of litres each month that just go down the drain. Watering plants during the day might seem helpful, but actually wastes water since the hot sun causes much of it to evaporate before the plants can use it - it's better to water early in the morning or evening. Remember, in Botswana where water is precious, every drop we save makes a difference!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the purpose of the ozone layer?

options.text: To keep the earth warm at night
To protect the earth from harmful UV rays
To reflect sunlight back into space

questions.explanation: The ozone layer is like a protective shield high up in our atmosphere that blocks harmful ultraviolet (UV) rays from the sun, which can cause skin cancer and damage our eyes. While the atmosphere does help keep Earth warm and contains oxygen, the ozone layer's special job is specifically to filter out these dangerous UV rays before they reach us. Think of it as Earth's natural sunscreen protecting all living things!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following correctly describes a second class lever?

options.text: Effort and load at the same end

questions.explanation: A second class lever always has the load positioned between the pivot (fulcrum) and the effort, which makes it easier to lift heavy things - just like when you use a wheelbarrow where the wheel is the pivot, the load sits in the middle, and you lift the handles with effort. You might have thought the pivot goes between effort and load (option A), but that describes a first class lever like a seesaw. Remember the "2" in second class means the load is in position number 2, right in the middle!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the name of the force that pulls objects towards the centre of the earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the force that pulls all objects downward towards the centre of the Earth, which is why when you drop something, it always falls down rather than floating away. Friction is a different force that happens when two surfaces rub against each other, like when you try to push a heavy box across the floor. Gravity keeps us on the ground and makes things fall when we let them go.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a non-renewable energy source?
questions.explanation: Coal is a non-renewable energy source because it formed from ancient plants buried deep underground millions of years ago, and once we use it up, it cannot be replaced in our lifetime. Wind, solar, and hydroelectric power are all renewable sources because they come from natural processes that keep happening - the sun keeps shining, wind keeps blowing, and water keeps flowing in rivers. Coal takes millions of years to form, so we must use it wisely while developing more renewable energy sources for Botswana's future.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What does a thermometer measure?
questions.explanation: A thermometer is an instrument that measures temperature, which tells us how hot or cold something is. You can use a thermometer to measure the temperature of the air, water, or even your body when you're sick. Wind speed is measured by a different instrument called an anemometer, not a thermometer.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a sign of puberty in girls?
questions.explanation: During puberty, girls experience several body changes, and one of the most common signs is the growth of pubic hair in the private areas of the body. While a deep voice, broader shoulders, and facial hair are changes that happen to boys during puberty, girls develop different characteristics like pubic hair growth, breast development, and the start of menstruation. These changes are all normal and happen because your body is growing and maturing.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the function of the ovaries in the female reproductive system?
questions.explanation: The ovaries are two small organs in a woman's body that make egg cells, which are needed to create a baby when they join with sperm. Some students might think the uterus (womb) is the ovary because that's where the baby grows, but the ovaries only make the eggs before they travel to the uterus. While ovaries do produce hormones, these hormones are for females, not for boys.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following describes fertilisation?

options.text: When an egg is released from the ovary
When a sperm and egg join together

questions.explanation: Fertilisation is the special moment when a sperm cell from the father joins together with an egg cell from the mother to start creating a new baby. This joining usually happens in the mother's fallopian tube after the egg is released from the ovary. Remember that releasing the egg (option A) happens before fertilisation, while the baby being born (option C) happens many months after fertilisation has already occurred.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a cause of teenage pregnancy?
questions.explanation: Peer pressure is a cause of teenage pregnancy because when friends or peers encourage teenagers to have sex before they are ready, some may give in even though they know the risks. Good parenting actually helps prevent teenage pregnancy because supportive parents teach their children about making responsible choices and waiting until they are older. Abstaining from sex and staying in school are both ways to prevent teenage pregnancy, not causes of it.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which animal is most protected in Botswana?
questions.explanation: Rhinos are the most protected animals in Botswana because they are critically endangered and face threats from poaching for their horns, which makes them need special armed guards and strict laws to keep them safe. While hippos are also protected as wild animals, they are not as endangered as rhinos and don't need the same high level of security. Cows and donkeys are domestic animals that people own, so they don't need government protection like endangered wildlife.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What contribution did Alexander Graham Bell make in science?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell was a famous inventor who created the telephone in 1876, which allowed people to talk to each other over long distances for the first time. While Thomas Edison invented the light bulb around the same time, Bell's important contribution to science was making voice communication possible through wires. The wheel was invented thousands of years before Bell was even born, and tourism is not an invention but an activity people do when they travel.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens at A? (SOLID ← A — LIQUID — B → GAS)
questions.explanation: When a solid changes into a liquid at point A, we call this process melting, like when ice cubes melt into water on a hot day. Boiling and evaporation are incorrect because these processes happen at point B where a liquid changes into a gas, not where a solid becomes a liquid.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The process at B is …
questions.explanation: When a liquid changes into a solid state, we call this process freezing, which is what happens at point B where the substance is becoming solid. You might think it's cooling because the temperature is dropping, but cooling just means losing heat and can happen in any state, while freezing is the specific change from liquid to solid. Remember, melting is the opposite (solid to liquid) and evaporation is when liquid becomes gas.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: The set-ups below show salt particles in water. In which set-up will the salt dissolve fastest?
questions.explanation: Salt dissolves fastest when two conditions are met: the water must be hot and the salt particles must be small. Hot water gives particles more energy to move and break apart the salt, while small particles have more surface area exposed to the water, allowing them to dissolve quickly. Although small particles in cold water (option A) will dissolve faster than large particles, the lack of heat slows down the process compared to hot water with small particles.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Kabo was asked to soften hard water which was collected from a stream. Which of the following set-ups can he use?
questions.explanation: Boiling hard water over fire is the correct method because heating removes the minerals like calcium and magnesium that make water hard. Filtering through cloth might seem right, but it only removes dirt and solid particles, not the dissolved minerals that cause hardness. When you boil hard water, the heat causes chemical changes that make those minerals settle out, leaving you with softer water.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What happens at a cold front?

options.text: Cold air sinks and warms up

questions.explanation: When a cold front moves through an area, a mass of cold air pushes forward and slides underneath the warm air that was there before, forcing the warm air to rise quickly. This means the cold air is replacing the warm air at ground level, which is why we feel a sudden drop in temperature when a cold front passes. While it's true that warm air does rise during this process (option B), that's what happens because of the cold front, not what the cold front itself is – a cold front is specifically about cold air moving in to take the place of warm air.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide is a greenhouse gas because it traps heat from the sun in Earth's atmosphere, like a blanket keeping our planet warm. Oxygen and nitrogen make up most of the air we breathe, but they don't trap heat the same way, which is why they are not greenhouse gases. When we burn fuels or cut down trees, we add more carbon dioxide to the air, making Earth warmer than it should be.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the name for the layer of gases surrounding the earth?
questions.explanation: The blanket of gases that wraps around our entire Earth is called the atmosphere. While the ozone layer, stratosphere, and troposphere are all important parts within the atmosphere, they are just individual layers, not the name for the whole covering of gases around our planet. Think of it like this: your school uniform has different parts like a shirt and trousers, but "uniform" is the name for the complete outfit, just as "atmosphere" is the name for all the gas layers together.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a way to protect yourself from lightning?
questions.explanation: Lightning is very dangerous and seeks the easiest path to the ground, so the safest place during a thunderstorm is indoors away from windows, where the building's structure protects you. Never stand under tall trees during lightning storms because lightning often strikes the tallest objects, and you could be hit or injured by the electrical current traveling through the tree. Open fields and metal objects are also extremely dangerous because they attract lightning strikes.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: What is the function of chlorophyll in plants?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green substance in plant leaves that captures energy from sunlight, which plants need to make their own food through photosynthesis. Without chlorophyll to absorb the sun's light energy, plants couldn't convert carbon dioxide and water into the sugar they need to grow. While roots do absorb water from soil, that's a different job done by root hairs, not by chlorophyll.

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is an example of a third class lever?
questions.explanation: Tweezers are a third class lever because the effort (where your fingers squeeze) is between the fulcrum (the joined end) and the load (what you're picking up). A wheelbarrow might seem similar, but it's actually a second class lever because the load is between the fulcrum (the wheel) and the effort (the handles you lift). Remember, in third class levers, you always apply force in the middle!

questions.subject: science
questions.question_text: Which of the following is a correct statement about electrical conductors?

options.text: Copper is a good conductor of electricity
Rubber is a good conductor of electricity
Wood is a good conductor of electricity

questions.explanation: Copper is indeed a good conductor of electricity, which is why it is commonly used in electrical wires throughout homes and buildings in Botswana and around the world. Metals like copper, iron, and aluminum allow electricity to flow through them easily, so option A is incorrect because most metals are actually good conductors, not poor ones. Materials like rubber and wood are insulators that do not allow electricity to pass through easily, which is why rubber is used to cover electrical wires to keep us safe.
